"He had already finished the project."
"I already finished the project."
"I have already finished the project."
explanation: A clean, standard reversal: 'had finished' (Past Perfect) reverses to 'have finished' (Present Perfect), and 'she' (third person, as reported by 'me') reverses to 'I' (since she is now speaking about herself directly). Option (a) incorrectly uses Future Perfect ('will have finished'), an unrelated tense. Option (b) fails to reverse the pronoun 'she' to 'I' and instead wrongly changes it to 'he'. Option (c) correctly reverses the pronoun but wrongly uses Simple Past 'finished' instead of the correct Present Perfect 'have finished' — remember, Past Perfect always reverses specifically to Present Perfect, never to Simple Past.

set8.word: Sakshi said that she would do it then or never. (Convert to DIRECT)
options: "I would do it then or never."
"I have to do it now or never."
"I will do it now or never."
"I will do it then or never."
explanation: This tests an important, often-overlooked time-word pair: 'then' — when it refers to a future point within the reported sentence, not a past point — reverses to 'now' once converted back to the moment of original speaking. Alongside this, 'would' reverses to 'will' as usual. Option (a) fails to reverse 'would' to 'will', leaving it in the backshifted form. Option (b) both fails to reverse the modal properly (introducing the unrelated 'have to') and misses updating 'then' appropriately. Option (d) correctly reverses 'would' to 'will' but wrongly keeps 'then' instead of the correct 'now' — remembering that time-and-place words reverse just as tenses do is essential in these reverse-conversion questions.

set8.word: He told me that he would have finished the project by the following day. (Convert to DIRECT)
options: "I have finished the project by tomorrow," he said to me.
"I would finish the project," he said to me tomorrow.
"I will finish the project by the next day," he said to me.
"I will have finished the project by tomorrow," he said to me.
explanation: 'Would have finished' (backshifted Future Perfect) reverses to 'will have finished' (Future Perfect) — note that Future Perfect is a distinct tense from Simple Future ('will finish'), and it must be reversed to its own matching form, not simplified into Simple Future. Also, 'the following day' (used when reporting later) reverses to 'tomorrow' (used at the original moment of speaking, referring to the day immediately after). Option (a) incorrectly drops the future auxiliary entirely, using Present Perfect instead. Option (b) fails to preserve the Future Perfect tense and misplaces the time reference. Option (c) uses Simple Future 'will finish' instead of the correctly-required Future Perfect 'will have finished', and also fails to fully reverse 'the following day' back to 'tomorrow'.

set8.word: "You were warned by us three days ago," the manager shouted at him. (Convert to INDIRECT)
options: ...that he was warned by them three days ago.
...that he was warned by them three days earlier.
...that he had been warned by them three days before.
...that he was warned by them three days before.
explanation: This forward-direction question tests the Passive Voice backshift alongside the crucial 'ago' to 'before' time-word change. 'Were warned' (Simple Past Passive) backshifts to 'had been warned' (Past Perfect Passive), since the warning happened even earlier than the moment of the manager's shouting — a clear 'past before a past' situation, exactly when Past Perfect must be used. Separately, 'three days ago' (a phrase only valid at the exact moment of the original speech) becomes 'three days before' once reported later — specifically 'before', not 'earlier', since 'before' is the standard word paired with 'ago' in Narration, while 'earlier' is used more for comparing two different times rather than marking distance from a reference point. Options (a) and (d) both fail to backshift the passive verb to Past Perfect. Option (b) correctly backshifts the verb but incorrectly uses 'earlier' instead of the standard 'before'.

set9.word: A sentence has been given in Indirect Speech. Choose the correct Direct Speech form. The explorer remarked that rarely had he seen such a breathtaking view in all his travels.
options: The explorer remarked, "I had rarely seen such a breathtaking view in all my travels."
The explorer remarked, "Rarely had I seen such a breathtaking view in all my travels."
The explorer remarked, "I rarely saw such a breathtaking view in all my travels."
The explorer remarked, "Rarely I had seen such a breathtaking view in all my travels."
explanation: जब कोई वाक्य 'Rarely', 'Seldom' जैसे Negative Adverbs से शुरू होता है, तो Inversion (Verb + Subject) का प्रयोग होता है। Indirect speech में भी 'rarely had he seen' (inversion) रखा गया है, तो Direct Speech में भी यह Inversion बरकरार रहेगा (Rarely had I seen).

set9.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: He observed that, had market liquidity vanished entirely, the exchange would have suspended trading.
options: He said, "If market liquidity vanishes entirely, the exchange suspends trading."
He said, "Had market liquidity vanished entirely, the exchange would have suspended trading."
He said, "The exchange will suspend trading if market liquidity had vanished entirely."
He said, "If the exchange suspended trading, it was because market liquidity vanished entirely."
explanation: Super Rule: 'Type 3 Conditional' (Had + Sub + V3) में Indirect से Direct बनाते समय (या वापस करते समय) Tense में कोई बदलाव नहीं होता है। Inversion (had market liquidity vanished) वैसा ही रहेगा।

set9.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: He speculated that, had stochastic volatility been incorporated, the valuation would perhaps have been more conservative.
options: He speculated, "Had stochastic volatility been incorporated, the valuation would perhaps have been more conservative."
He speculated, "If stochastic volatility is incorporated, valuation will perhaps be conservative."
He speculated, "If volatility had been incorporated, valuation perhaps more conservative."
He said, "Stochastic volatility incorporation would make valuation conservative."
explanation: फिर से Type 3 Conditional Inversion! 'Had + Sub + V3' और 'would have + V3' का स्ट्रक्चर Direct Speech में 100% अपरिवर्तित (unchanged) रहता है।

set9.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The diplomat said that only after the ceasefire would negotiations begin.
options: "Negotiations begin only after the ceasefire," said the diplomat.
"Only after the ceasefire, negotiations would begin," said the diplomat.
"Only after the ceasefire will negotiations begin," said the diplomat.
"Negotiations would begin once the ceasefire is announced," said the diplomat.
explanation: 'Only after' से शुरू होने वाले वाक्यों में भी Inversion लगता है। Indirect में would negotiations begin है, तो Direct में would वापस will में बदल जाएगा और Inversion (will negotiations begin) बरकरार रहेगा।

set9.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The composer explained that, were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely.
options: The composer said, "If the leitmotif was transposed an octave higher, the tension will dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "Were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "The tension will dissipate prematurely if the leitmotif had been transposed an octave higher."
The composer said, "Had the leitmotif been transposed an octave higher, the tension must have dissipated prematurely."
explanation: यह Type 2 Conditional (Subjunctive Mood) का Inversion है। काल्पनिक (Unreal) स्थिति दर्शाने वाले "Were + Subject" वाले वाक्यों के Tense में Indirect और Direct Speech के बीच कोई बदलाव नहीं होता।

set9.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The professor said that had he known earlier, he would have made the necessary arrangements.
options: "If I knew earlier, I would have made the necessary arrangements," said the professor.
"Had I known earlier, I would have made the necessary arrangements," said the professor.
"If I would have known earlier, I would make arrangements," the professor said.
"Had I knew earlier, I would arrange things," said the professor.
explanation: 'Had he known' (Type 3 Inversion) Direct Speech में 'Had I known' रहेगा। Tense backshift नहीं होगा, के वल Pronoun 'he' वापस 'I' में बदल जाएगा।

set9.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: He confessed that the violin concerto would have remained fragmentary had the late maestro not bequeathed his annotated sketches.
options: He said, "The violin concerto would have remained fragmentary had the late maestro not bequeathed his annotated sketches."
He said, "The violin concerto would have remained fragmentary if the late maestro not bequeathed his annotated sketches."
He said, "The violin concerto will be remaining fragmentary had the late maestro not bequeathed his annotated sketches."
He said, "The violin concerto would be fragmentary if sketches had not been bequeathed."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional (Unreal Past). would have remained और had + not + bequeathed में कोई Tense Change नहीं होगा।

set9.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The lawyer said that had the evidence ot been tampered with, the verdict could have been n different.
options: "If the evidence had not tampered with, the verdict could change," said the lawyer.
"If the evidence were not tampered with, the verdict could have changed," the lawyer said.
"Had the evidence not been tampered with, the verdict could have been different," said the lawyer.
"Had not the evidence tampered, the verdict may differ," said he.
explanation: Inversion (had the evidence not been tampered with) और Modal perfect (could have been) दोनों Direct Speech में जस के तस बने रहेंगे।

set9.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: He remarked that the sculpture would have commanded greater gravitas had the pedestal been proportionally austere.
options: He said, "The sculpture would have commanded greater gravitas had the pedestal been proportionally austere."
He said, "If the pedestal is proportionally austere, the sculpture commands greater gravitas."
He said, "The sculpture will command greater gravitas if the pedestal had been austere."
He said, "Had the pedestal been austere, the sculpture must have commanded greater gravitas."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional Inversion. would have commanded और had the pedestal been ज्यों के त्यों रहेंगे।

set9.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The analyst said that were the policies amended earlier, the crisis could have been avoided.
options: "If the policies were amended earlier, the crisis could have been avoided," said the analyst.
"Had the policies been amended earlier, the crisis could have been avoided," said the analyst.
"If only the policies would be amended, the crisis was avoided," he said.
"Had we amended the policies, we avoided the crisis," the analyst said.
explanation: ध्यान दें! Indirect में "were the policies amended" (Type 2 Subjunctive) और "could have been avoided" (Type 3) का मिश्रण है, जो कि असल में Past Impossible (Type 3) की ओर इशारा करता है। Direct में इसे पूरी तरह से Type 3 Inversion "Had the policies been amended" में ठीक किया गया है।

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: He commented that the policy's efficacy would remain conjectural until longitudinal data accrued.
options: He commented "The efficacy of the policy remains conjectural as the data are yet to be collected."
He commented, "The policy's efficacy will remain conjectural until longitudinal data accrue."
He commented, "Until longitudinal data had accrued, the policy's efficacy might have remained conjectural."
He commented, "Efficacy remains conjecture till data accumulates."
explanation: Indirect में would है, जो Direct में will बनेगा। accrued (Simple Past) वापस accrue (Simple Present) में बदल जाएगा।

set10.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: He said that little did he realize what consequences his words would have.
options: "Little do I realize what consequences my words would have," he said.
"Little did I realize what consequences my words would have," he said.
"I realized little about the consequences of my words," he said.
"I hardly realize the consequences of my words," said he.
explanation: Indirect में "little did he realize" (Past Inversion) है। इसका अर्थ है कि Direct Speech में यह Present Inversion "Little do I realize" रहा होगा। Modal 'would have' में कोई बदलाव नहीं होगा।

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: She indicated that, unless the quorum were met promptly, the charter amendments would lapse.
options: She said, "Unless the quorum had been met promptly, the charter amendments would have lapsed."
She said, "Unless the quorum is met promptly, the charter amendments will lapse."
She said, "If the quorum was not met promptly, the charter amendments might have lapsed."
She said, "If the quorum is promptly met, the charter amendments must not lapse."
explanation: Indirect में 'were met... would lapse' (Past Conditional) है। Direct Speech में यह Present-Future Conditional (First Conditional) में वापस आ जाएगा: is met... will lapse।

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: She posited that the narrative would have achieved greater cohesion had the author maintained a single focalisation.
options: She said, "If the author maintains a single focalisation, the narrative will achieve greater cohesion."
She said, "The narrative would have achieved greater cohesion had the author maintained a single focalisation."
She said, "The narrative will achieve greater cohesion if a single focalisation was maintained by the author."
She said, "Had the author maintained a single focalisation, the narrative must achieve greater cohesion."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional. No tense backshift/forward shift.

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The ethicist argued that, absent informed consent, the study's methodology was fundamentally untenable.
options: The ethicist argued, "Absent informed consent, the study's methodology is fundamentally untenable."
The ethicist argued, "If the study lacked informed consent, its methodology had been fundamentally untenable."
The ethicist argued, "Without informed consent, the study's methodology would have been fundamentally untenable."
The ethicist argued, "The study's methodology must have been untenable since it lacked informed consent."
explanation: Indirect में was है, जो Direct में वापस is हो जाएगा। "Absent informed consent" एक Formal Absolute Phrase है जो अपरिवर्तित रहेगा।

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: She noted that, had the literary trope not been overused, the metaphor might still have resonated with the audience.
options: She said, "If the literary trope was not overused, the metaphor may still resonate with the audience."
She said, "Had the literary trope not been overused, the metaphor might still have resonated with the audience."
She said, "Had the literary trope not been overused, the metaphor must still resonate with the audience."
She said, "If the literary trope had not been overused, the metaphor will still resonate with the audience."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional Inversion. No tense change.

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: He asserted that the cryptographic protocol might have resisted the attack had entropy been truly random.
options: He asserted, "Had entropy been truly random, the cryptographic protocol might have resisted the attack."
He said, "If entropy is truly random, protocol might resist attack."
He asserted, "If entropy had been random, protocol might resist attack."
He said, "Entropy random, protocol resists attack."
explanation: Unreal Past Conditional (Type 3). 'might have resisted' और 'had entropy been' (Inversion) बिल्कु ल वैसा ही रहेगा।

set10.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The composer explained that, were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely.
options: The composer said, "If the leitmotif was transposed an octave higher, the tension will dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "Were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "The tension will dissipate prematurely if the leitmotif had been transposed an octave higher."
The composer said, "Had the leitmotif been transposed an octave higher, the tension must have dissipated prematurely."
explanation: Type 2 Subjunctive Inversion (were the leitmotif transposed). काल्पनिक स्थिति होने के कारण Direct Speech में भी Tense समान रहेगा।

set10.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: He said that if he were to receive a promotion, he would buy his family a bigger house.
options: He said, "If I received a promotion, I would buy my family a bigger house."
He said, "If I were to receive a promotion, I would buy my family a bigger house."
He said, "If I receive a promotion, I will buy my family a bigger house."
He said, "If I had received a promotion, I would have bought my family a bigger house."
explanation: "If I were to receive" एक Formal Conditional (Subjunctive) है। अवास्तविक (Unreal) कल्पना वाले वाक्यों का Tense Direct Speech में नहीं बदलता।

set10.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The historian said, "Even if the war had ended arlier, the long-term economic effects would have been e equally devastating for the country."
options: The historian said that even if the war would have ended earlier...
The historian said that even if the war had ended earlier, the long-term economic effects would have been equally devastating for the country.
The historian said that even if the war ended earlier...
The historian said that even if the war had ended earlier, the long-term economic effects will have been equally devastating...
explanation: Direct Speech में पहले से ही Type 3 Conditional (had ended / would have been) है। Indirect बनाते समय इसमें कोई बैकशिफ्ट नहीं होता।

set11.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The primatologist pointed out that grooming behaviour would diminish if the troop hierarchy were destabilised.
options: The primatologist said, "Grooming behaviour would diminish if the troop hierarchy were destabilised."
The primatologist said, "Grooming behaviour will be diminished if the troop hierarchy is destabilised."
The primatologist said, "If the troop hierarchy had been destabilised, grooming behaviour would have diminished."
The primatologist said, "Were the troop hierarchy destabilised, grooming behaviour must diminish."
explanation: 'would diminish' और 'were destabilised' Type 2 Conditional हैं। Direct Speech में ये नहीं बदलते।

set11.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: She observed that the appellate bench might overturn the ruling were new exculpatory evidence to surface.
options: She observed, "The appellate bench may overturn the ruling if new exculpatory evidence surfaces."
She said, "The appellate bench might overturn the ruling were new exculpatory evidence to surface."
She observed, "Should new exculpatory evidence surface, the bench has overturned the ruling."
She said, "Evidence surfaces, ruling might overturn."
explanation: यहाँ might overturn को may overturn में और Formal Inversion were... to surface को First Conditional if... surfaces में वापस लाया गया है (Real condition)।

set11.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The archivist declared that, unless the humidity controls were recalibrated, the papyri could deteriorate beyond salvage.
options: The archivist declared, "Unless the humidity controls are recalibrated, the papyri could deteriorate beyond salvage."
The archivist declared, "If humidity controls were recalibrated, papyri would deteriorate."
The archivist declared, "Unless humidity controls were recalibrated, the papyri could deteriorate beyond salvage."
The archivist declared, "Unless humidity controls were recalibrated, papyri can get deteriorated beyond salvage."
explanation: Indirect में were recalibrated (Past) वापस are recalibrated (Present) हो जाएगा। Modal could को डायरे क्ट स्पीच में भी 'could' के रूप में (भविष्य की संभावना के लिए) इस्तेमाल किया जा सकता है।

set11.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The official said that only if the forms were submitted on time would the applications be processed.
options: "The applications would be processed only if the forms were submitted on time," said the official.
"Only if the forms were submitted on time would the applications be processed," said the official.
"If forms are submitted on time, the applications will be processed," said the official.
"Submit forms on time and your applications will be processed," said the official.
explanation: "Only if" से शुरू होने वाला क्लॉज़ जब वाक्य के शुरू में आता है तो Inversion लेता है (would the applications be processed)। यह Formal Conditional Inversion डायरे क्ट स्पीच में भी बिल्कु ल वैसा ही रहता है।

set11.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: The philosopher remarked that, had the syllogism been valid, the paradox would not have arisen.
options: The philosopher said, "If the syllogism is valid, the paradox does not arise."
The philosopher said, "Had the syllogism been valid, the paradox would not arise."
The philosopher said, "Had the syllogism been valid, the paradox would not have arisen."
The philosopher said, "If syllogism is valid, paradox should not arise."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional Inversion. कोई बदलाव नहीं होगा।

set11.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the correct direct speech: He mused that, were the glacier to retreat another kilometre, the valley ecosystem could irrevocably shift.
options: He mused, "If the glacier retreats another kilometre, the valley ecosystem could irrevocably shift."
He mused, "Were the glacier to retreat another kilometre, the valley ecosystem could irrevocably shift."
He said, "If glacier retreated another kilometre, ecosystem would shift."
He mused, "Had the glacier retreated another kilometre, ecosystem could shift."
explanation: "Were the glacier to retreat" एक Subjunctive Inversion है जो काल्पनिक स्थिति दर्शाता है । यह Direct Speech में नहीं बदलता।

set11.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech. He warned that if we did not act immediately, the consequences would be irreversible.
options: He said, "If you didn't act immediately, the consequences will be irreversible."
He said, "If you don't act immediately, the consequences will be irreversible."
He said, "If you hadn't act immediately, the consequences would be irreversible."
He said, "If you haven't act immediately, the consequences would be irreversible."
explanation: Indirect में did not act (Past) और would be (Past Modal) है। Direct Speech में यह First Conditional में वापस आ जाएगा: don't act और will be।

set11.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech. The engineer pointed out that, by the time the backup system activated, the damage would already have been done.
options: The engineer pointed out, "By the time the backup system activates, the damage will already have been done."
The engineer said, "By the time the backup system is activated, the damage will already be done."
The engineer pointed out, "By the time the backup system activated, the damage had already been done."
The engineer said, "By the time the backup system was activated, the damage would have already been done"
explanation: 'activated' (Simple Past) वापस 'activates' (Simple Present) में बदल जाएगा और 'would already have been' वापस 'will already have been' (Future Perfect) में आ जाएगा।

set11.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech. She said that she could not find her necklace anywhere and fears it might have been stolen.
options: She said, "I didn't find my necklace anywhere. I fear it could have been stolen."
She said, "I couldn't find my necklace anywhere and fears it might be stolen."
She said, "I can't find my necklace anywhere. I fear it may have been stolen."
She said, "I could find my necklace nowhere and fear it might have been stolen."
explanation: could not वापस can't में, और might have been वापस may have been में बदलेगा। चूँकि Indirect में fears (present tense) है, इसका मतलब Direct Speech में भी fear (present) रहा होगा।

set11.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Manohar said to Sunaina, "The manager had a helicopter last year, but he sold it to meet his expenses."
options: Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had a helicopter last year but he had sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had had a helicopter the previous year but he sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had had a helicopter the previous year but he had sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had a helicopter last year but he has sold it to meet his expenses.
explanation: Direct में 'had' (रखता था - Simple Past V2) है, जो Indirect में Past Perfect (had had) हो जाएगा। 'sold' (V2) बदलकर 'had sold' हो जाएगा। 'Last year' बदलकर 'the previous year' हो जाएगा।

set12.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Rakesh said to Ranjana, "This book was purchased by my father when I was young."
options: Rakesh told Ranjana that book was purchased by his father when he was young.
Rakesh told Ranjana that that book was purchased by my father when he was young.
Rakesh told Ranjana that that book had purchased by his father when he had young.
Rakesh told Ranjana that that book had been purchased by his father when he had been young.
explanation: 'This' -> 'that' में बदलेगा। 'was purchased' (Simple Past Passive) -> 'had been urchased' (Past Perfect Passive)। 'was young' (Simple Past) p -> 'had been young' (Past Perfect)।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the indirect speech: "Though the facts were evident, the jury chose to ignore them," he observed.
options: He observed the facts had been evident, but jury ignored them.
He observed that though the facts had been evident, the jury had chosen to ignore them.
He observed though facts were evident, jury ignored it.
He observed that even though facts were evident, jury ignored them.
explanation: Simple Past were evident और chose दोनों Past Perfect में बैकशिफ्ट होंगे: had been evident और had chosen।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: The professor reminded the students that if they had not registered on the portal by midnight, their submissions would not be accepted.
options: "Your submissions will not be accepted if you don't register by midnight," the professor reminded them.
"If you haven't registered by midnight, your work won't be accepted," the professor warned.
"Register on the portal by midnight or your submissions will be rejected," the professor said.
"If you fail to register by midnight, your submissions won't be accepted," said the professor.
explanation: Indirect में had not registered और would not be accepted है। Direct Speech में यह First Conditional होगा। (d) इसका सबसे बेहतरीन और सटीक रूपांतरण है जहाँ 'fail to register' का इस्तेमाल नकारात्मक शर्त के लिए किया गया है।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: She asked whether, in view of the delays caused by external vendors, the timeline for delivery could be reconsidered.
options: "Could the delivery timeline be reconsidered because of the delays by external vendors?" she asked.
"Given the vendor delays, can you review the delivery schedule?" she asked.
"Would it be possible to reconsider the timeline due to the external delays?" she asked.
"Is there any chance the delivery deadline can be extended due to the vendor delays?" she asked.
explanation: Indirect में Passive Voice 'could be reconsidered' है, तो Direct में भी Passive Voice ही रहेगा 'Could the delivery timeline be reconsidered...'।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the indirect speech: "Let it be known that we shall defend every inch of our territory," proclaimed the commander.
options: The commander proclaimed they would defend every inch of their territory.
The commander proclaimed that let it be known they shall defend territory.
The commander declared that every inch of land would be defended.
The commander proclaimed that they would defend every inch of their territory.
explanation: 'Let it be known' एक औपचारिक घोषणा (Proclamation) का स्टाइल है। Reporting verb proclaimed इस भाव को संभाल लेता है, इसलिए 'let it be known' को हटा दिया जाता है। 'we shall' -> 'they would' में बदल जाएगा।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the indirect speech: "So great was his shock that he could barely utter a word," said the nurse.
options: The nurse said he was shocked greatly and barely spoke.
The nurse said his shock was so great that he had barely spoken.
The nurse said that so great had his shock been that he could barely utter a word.
The nurse said that his shock was so great that he could barely utter a word.
explanation: Direct Speech में "So great was his shock" (Inversion for emphasis) का प्रयोग हुआ है। Indirect में इसे साधारण वाक्य (Assertive order) "his shock was so great" में लिखा जाता है। चूँकि नर्स एक पास्ट की स्थिति (state) बता रही है , 'was' को 'had been' में बैकशिफ्ट करना ज़रूरी नहीं है।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the indirect speech: "If you had followed my advice, you would not be in this mess now," she said angrily.
options: She said angrily that if I had followed her advice, I wouldn't be in this mess now.
She angrily said if I followed her advice, I would not be in this mess.
She angrily said that if I had followed her advice, I would not be in that mess then.
She angrily said that if I had followed her advice, I would not have been in this mess.
explanation: Mixed Conditional! had followed (Type 3) और would not be (Type 2)। Conditionals बैकशिफ्ट नहीं होते। सिर्फ Pronoun (you -> I), Demonstrative (this mess -> that mess), और Time (now -> then) बदलेगा।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the indirect speech: "Might I suggest a revision to clause three?" the lawyer inquired politely.
options: The lawyer suggested to revise clause three.
The lawyer politely asked might he suggest a revision.
The lawyer politely inquired if he might suggest a revision to clause three.
The lawyer advised revising clause three.
explanation: "Might I..." एक बहुत ही विनम्र प्रश्न है। Indirect में if/whether लगेगा और Subject-Verb order सीधा हो जाएगा (he might)।

set12.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: The analyst commented that had the model incorporated volatility, projections would have been accurate.
options: "Had the model incorporated volatility, projections would have been accurate," commented the analyst.
"Projections can never be accurate," said the analyst.
"Why use volatility in models at all?" asked the analyst.
"We removed volatility to simplify projections," said the analyst.
explanation: Type 3 Conditional Inversion. Direct Speech में कोई बदलाव नहीं होगा।

set12.word: Select the correct indirect form: "Do you suppose you know better than your own father?" jeered his angry mother.
options: His angry mother jeered and asked if you suppose you know better than your own father.
His angry mother jeered and asked whether he had supposed he has known better than his own father.
His angry mother jeered that did he suppose he knew better than his own father.
His angry mother jeered and asked whether he supposed he knew better than his own father.
explanation: Present Tense do you suppose -> Past he supposed, you know -> he knew। Interrogative वाक्य Assertive में बदल जाएगा (whether he supposed he knew)।

set13.word: Choose the correct indirect speech: The labourers said to the contractor, "Did you ask the manager when we are going to receive our remuneration that has been pending for the last three months?"
options: The labourers asked the contractor if he asked the manager when they were going to receive their remuneration that had been pending for the previous three months.
The labourers asked the contractor if he had asked the manager when they were going to receive their remuneration that had been pending for the previous three months.
The labourers asked the contractor if he had asked the manager when they are going to receive...
The labourers asked the contractor if had he asked the manager...
explanation: इस बड़े वाक्य में 4 बदलाव एक साथ हो रहे हैं: 1. Did you ask (Simple Past) → had asked (Past Perfect). . are going to (Present Continuous) → were going to (Past 2 Continuous). . has been pending (Present Perfect Continuous) → had 3 been pending (Past Perfect Continuous). 4. last three months → previous three months. ऑप्शन (b) इन चारों नियमों का 100% सटीक पालन करता है।

set13.word: Choose the correct direct speech form: The farmer asked his wife why she was upset when he had just returned from the city.
options: The farmer said to his wife, "Why are you so upset when I just returned from the city?"
The farmer said to his wife, "Why did you upset when I returned from the city?"
The farmer said to his wife, "Why are you upset when I have just returned from the city?"
The farmer said to his wife, "Why are you upset when I was just returned from the city?"
explanation: Indirect से Direct बनाना है। was upset (Simple Past) वापस are you upset (Present) में बदलेगा। had just returned (Past Perfect) वापस have just returned (Present Perfect) में बदलेगा। 'she' → 'you' और 'he' → 'I' हो जाएगा।

set13.word: Choose the correct Direct Speech form: The climatologist said that if global temperatures kept rising, sea levels would threaten major coastal cities.
options: The climatologist said, "If global temperatures kept rising, sea levels will threaten major coastal cities."
The climatologist said, "If global temperatures had kept rising, sea levels would have threatened major coastal cities."
The climatologist said, "If global temperatures kept rising, sea levels would threaten major coastal cities."
The climatologist said, "If global temperatures keep rising, sea levels will threaten major coastal cities."
explanation: Indirect वाक्य में kept (V2) और would है। इसका मतलब है कि Direct Speech में यह Type 1 Conditional रहा होगा। इसलिए V2 (kept) वापस V1 (keep) में और would वापस will में बदल जाएगा।

set13.word: Choose the correct Direct Speech form: The analyst stated that inflation could have been controlled if monetary policies had been revised earlier.
options: The analyst stated, "Inflation could have been controlled if monetary policies had been revised earlier."
The analyst stated, "Inflation could be controlled if monetary policies were revised earlier."
The analyst stated, "Inflation had been controlled if monetary policies had been revised earlier."
The analyst stated, "Inflation can be controlled if monetary policies are revised earlier."
explanation: वाक्य में could have been और had been revised (Type 3 Conditional) है। Type 3 Conditional के Tense में Indirect और Direct Speech के बीच कोई बदलाव नहीं होता है। वाक्य बिल्कु ल वैसा ही रहेगा।

set13.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The zoologist warned that the island's carrying capacity might be exceeded should invasive species proliferate unchecked.
options: The zoologist said, "The island's carrying capacity will be exceeded if invasive species proliferate unchecked."
The zoologist said, "Should invasive species have proliferated unchecked, the island's carrying capacity would be exceeded."
The zoologist said, "The island's carrying capacity might be exceeded should invasive species proliferate unchecked."
The zoologist said, "If invasive species proliferated unchecked, the carrying capacity had been exceeded."
explanation: Indirect में यह एक Conditional Warning (चेतावनी) है (might be + should... proliferate)। Direct Speech में इसे वास्तविक (Realistic) चेतावनी के रूप में will be और if... proliferate (Type 1 Conditional) में लिखा जाएगा।

set13.word: Choose the correct direct speech: The envoy reported that the negotiations were languishing because neither party would concede procedural precedence.
options: The envoy reported, "The negotiations languish because neither party would concede procedural precedence."
The envoy said, "The negotiations are languishing because neither party will be conceding procedural precedence."
The envoy said, "The negotiations are languishing because neither party will concede procedural precedence."
The envoy said, "Negotiations languished, no party conceding precedence."
explanation: were languishing (Past Continuous) वापस are languishing (Present Continuous) हो जाएगा। Modal would वापस will में बदल जाएगा।

set13.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The student said, "I have been practicing for the competition for months, but I am still nervous, as the other competitors are very skilled."
options: The student said that he has been practicing... but he is still nervous...
The student said that he had been practicing for the competition for months, but he was still nervous, as the other competitors were very skilled.
The student said that he had been practicing... but he is still nervous...
The student said that he was practicing...
explanation: 3 बड़े बदलाव: 1. have been practicing → had been practicing. 2. am → was. 3. are → were. एकमात्र ऑप्शन है जिसमें तीनों Tenses को सही ढंग से पास्ट में बैकशिफ्ट किया गया है।

set13.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The CEO announced that the company had lost a significant amount of revenue last year and that they were making difficult decisions to stay afloat.
options: "The company lost a significant amount of revenue last year, and we are making difficult decisions to stay afloat," the CEO announced.
"The company had lost..."
"The company loses..."
"The company lost... the previous year..."
explanation: Indirect में had lost और last year लिखा है। ध्यान दें: चूँकि Indirect में 'last year' को नहीं दला गया, इसका मतलब Direct Speech में भी last year ही था। ब इसलिए had lost वापस Simple Past lost में बदलेगा। were making वापस are making हो जाएगा और they वापस we बन जाएगा।

set13.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: He will tell her, "I have already finished my work."
options: He will tell her that he had already finished his work.
He will tell her that he has already finished his work.
He will tell her that he already finished his work.
He will tell her that he would have finished his work.
explanation: Super Rule: जब Reporting Verb Future Tense (will tell) या Present Tense में हो, तो Reported Speech के Tense में कोई बदलाव (Backshift) नहीं होता है। have finished बस Subject (he) के अनुसार has finished हो जाएगा।

set13.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The manager told the team that their recent performance had been impressive, but that they had to now focus on the upcoming project which was a much bigger challenge, and he was confident that they would succeed in it.
options: "Your recent performance was impressive, but you must now focus..."

"Your recent performance has been impressive, but you have to now focus on the upcoming project which is a much bigger challenge, and I am confident that you will succeed in it," the manager said to the team.
"Your recent performance has been impressive, but you must now focus..."
"Their recent performance was impressive..."
explanation: 5 बदलाव एक साथ: had been → has been | had to → have to | was → is | was confident → am confident | would → will। ऑप्शन (b) में सभी शब्दों को बिल्कु ल सही प्रेजेंट टेंस में रीस्टोर किया गया है।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The librarian told the child, "You should not be making so much noise, because the other students have been trying to study for their exams."
options: The librarian told the child that he should not be making so much noise, because the other students had been trying to study for their exams.
The librarian told the child that he should not have been making...
The librarian told the child that he would not be making...
The librarian told the child that he should not be making... have been trying...
explanation: Modal should (सलाह/बाध्यता) Indirect Speech में नहीं बदलता। सिर्फ have been trying बदलकर had been trying हो जाएगा।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The company CEO announced, "We will not be able to meet the deadline for this quarter, which is a great disappointment for all of us, but we will have to work harder next time."
options: The company CEO announced that they will not be able...
...that they would not be able to meet the deadline for this quarter...
The company CEO announced that they would not be able to meet the deadline for that quarter, which was a great disappointment for all of them, but that they would have to work harder next time.
...but that they would have to work harder the next time.
explanation: will → would, this → that, is → was, us → them, will → would। 'Next time' (अगली बार) एक अनिश्चित भविष्य की घटना है, इसलिए इसे 'the following time' में बदलना ज़रूरी नहीं होता, यह 'next time' ही रहता है ।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The captain explained that the ship had been ailing for three weeks and they had faced a severe storm the s previous night.
options: The captain explained, "The ship was sailing... last night."
The captain explained, "The ship has been sailing... had faced... last night."
The captain explained, "The ship has been sailing for three weeks, and we faced a severe storm last night."
The captain explained, "The ship had been sailing... faced... last night."
explanation: had been sailing (Past Perfect Continuous) → has been sailing (Present Perfect Continuous)। had faced (Past Perfect) → faced (Simple Past)। the previous night → last night।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: He asked me if I would have been able to finish the report that day if he had not helped me with the research, which had been a very tedious process.
options: "Would you have been able to finish the report today if I had not helped you with the research, which was a very tedious process?" he asked me.
"Will you have been able to..."
"Would you have been able... that day... had been..."
"Could you finish..."
explanation: Type 3 Conditional (would have been able + had not helped) Direct Speech में नहीं बदलता। that day → today। had been (Past Perfect) → was (Simple Past)।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: He said to her, "If you can solve this puzzle, I will give you the prize that I was planning to give away all along."
options: He told her that if she could solve that puzzle, he would give her the prize that he had been planning to give away all along.
He told her that if she can solve...
He told her that if she can solve...
He told her that if she could solve that puzzle, he would give her the prize that he had been planning to give away all along.
explanation: 4 बदलाव: can → could | this → that | will → would | was planning (Past Continuous) → had been planning (Past Perfect Continuous)।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The detective asked the suspect how he could ave committed the crime without leaving any evidence when h the security cameras had been recording all night.
options: "How can you commit..."
"How could you have committed... had been recording..."
The detective asked the suspect, "How could you have committed the crime without leaving any evidence when the security cameras were recording all night?"
"How can you have committed..."
explanation: how he could have → How could you have (Subject-Verb Inversion for Question)। had been recording (Past Perfect Continuous) → were recording (Past Continuous)।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The captain said, "We have been sailing in these choppy waters for two days, and we expect that the storm, which is now on the horizon, will hit us in a few hours."
options: The captain said that they have been sailing...
The captain said that they had been sailing in those choppy waters for two days, and they expected that the storm, which was then on the horizon, would hit them in a few hours.
...and they expected that the storm, which was then...
...and they expected that the storm, which is now...
explanation: 6 भारी बदलाव! have been had been | these → those | expect → expected | is → was | now → then | will → would। ऑप्शन (b) सब कु छ → सटीक करता है।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice. The scientist announced that the new compound would react violently if it came into contact with water, and that they had been observing its properties for months.
options: The scientist announced, "The new compound will react violently if it comes into contact with water, and we have been observing its properties for months."
"...will react violently if it came..."
"...would react violently if it came..."
"...will react violently if it comes... and we had been observing..."
explanation: would react → will react और came → comes (यह एक वैज्ञानिक तथ्य / Type 1 Conditional है)। had been observing → have been observing।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice. The judge said that the defendant's plea of not guilty had been a waste of the court's time, as the evidence hich had been presented by the prosecution had clearly w proven that he was guilty.
options: "...was a waste... was presented... has clearly proven... is guilty"
"...has been a waste... was presented... has clearly proven... is guilty"
"...had been a waste... was presented... had clearly proven... was guilty"
"The defendant's plea of not guilty has been a waste of the court's time, as the evidence which was presented by the prosecution had clearly proven that he was guilty," the judge said.
explanation: had been a waste → has been a waste। लेकिन Relative Clause के अंदर के Past Perfect (had clearly proven) को Direct Speech में भी Past Perfect ही रखा जाता है क्योंकि यह 'Past in the Past' एक्शन दिखाता है ।

set14.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The tour guide said, "We have been walking for hours, but the ruins we have been looking for are just around this corner, and we will be there in a few minutes."
options: The tour guide said that they had been walking for hours, but the ruins they had been looking for were just around that corner, and they would be there in a few minutes.
...that we have been walking...
...are just around that corner...
...that they were walking...
explanation: 5 बदलाव: have been → had been (दो बार) | are → were | this → that | will → would | We → they।

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The professor explained that although the theory had been considered correct for centuries, new findings now proved that it was flawed.
options: "...had been considered... now prove that it is flawed."
"...was considered... now prove that it is flawed."
The professor explained, "Although the theory has been considered correct for centuries, new findings now prove that it is flawed."
"...was considered... then proved that it was flawed."
explanation: had been considered → has been considered (Present Perfect Passive)। proved → prove (Simple Present)। was flawed → is flawed।

set15.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: The professor said, "The students who had been absent yesterday will have to submit their assignments by tomorrow, or they will be penalized."
options: The professor said that the students who had been absent the day before would have to submit their assignments by the next day, or they would be penalized.
The professor said that the students who were absent yesterday would have to submit their assignments by tomorrow, or they will be penalized.
The professor said that the students who had been absent the day before will have to submit their assignments by tomorrow, or they will be penalized.
The professor said that the students who were absent the day before would have to submit their assignments by the next day, or they would be penalized.
explanation: 1. had been absent (Past Perfect) stays unchanged in Past Perfect. 2. yesterday → the day before. 3. will have to (Future modal) → would have to (Past modal). 4. tomorrow → the next day. 5. will be penalized → would be penalized.

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: He told me, "I was trying to call you, but my phone's battery died, so I have been looking for a public phone since then."
options: He told me that he was trying to call me, but his phone's battery died, so he had been looking for a public phone since then.
He told me that he had been trying to call me, but his phone's battery had died, so he had been looking for a public phone since then.
He told me that he had been trying to call me, but his phone's battery died, so he had been looking for a public phone since then.
He told me that he had been trying to call me, but his phone's battery had died, so he has been looking for a public phone since then.
explanation: . was trying → had been trying (Past Continuous to Past 1 Perfect Continuous). 2. died → had died (Simple Past to Past Perfect). . have been looking → had been looking (Present Perfect 3 Continuous to Past Perfect Continuous). सभी वर्ब्स को एक-एक स्टेप पास्ट में ले जाना अनिवार्य है।

set15.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The composer explained that, were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely.
options: The composer said, "If the leitmotif was transposed an octave higher, the tension will dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "Were the leitmotif transposed an octave higher, the tension might dissipate prematurely."
The composer said, "The tension will dissipate prematurely if the leitmotif had been transposed an octave higher."
The composer said, "Had the leitmotif been transposed an octave higher, the tension must have dissipated prematurely."
explanation: यह एक काल्पनिक स्थिति (Type 2 Subjunctive) है। ऐसे वाक्यों का Tense Indirect से Direct में बदलते समय बदलता नहीं है।

set15.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: He told her that the documents had already been signed before she had called.
options: He said, "The documents were already signed before you called."
He said to her, "The documents have already been signed before you had called."
He said to her, "The documents had already been signed before you called."
He told her, "The documents have already been signed before you have called."
explanation: Past Perfect (had been signed + had called) को Direct Speech में भी Past Perfect में ही रखा जाता है ताकि समय का सही क्रम बना रहे।

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The librarian told the child, "You should not be making so much noise, because the other students have been trying to study for their exams."
options: The librarian told the child that he should not be making so much noise, because the other students had been trying to study for their exams.
The librarian told the child that he should not have been making so much noise, because the other students have been trying to study for their exams.
The librarian told the child that he would not be making so much noise...
The librarian told the child that he should not be making so much noise, because the other students have been trying...
explanation: have been trying (Present Perfect Continuous) → had been trying (Past Perfect Continuous)। Modal should अपरिवर्तित रहेगा।

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct Speech: The foreman stated that the team had completed the construction project the previous day.
options: The foreman said, "The team has completed the construction project yesterday."
The foreman stated, "The team had completed the construction project yesterday."
The foreman said, "The team completed the construction project yesterday."
The foreman said, "The team completed the construction project the previous day."
explanation: Indirect में had completed (Past Perfect) है, जो Direct में completed (Simple Past) हो जाता है। the previous day → yesterday।

set15.word: Choose the correct indirect narration of the following statement from the given alternatives: Manohar said to Sunaina, "The manager had a helicopter last year, but he sold it to meet his expenses."
options: Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had a helicopter last year but he had sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had had a helicopter the previous year but he sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had had a helicopter the previous year but he had sold it to meet his expenses.
Manohar told Sunaina that the manager had a helicopter last year but he has sold it to meet his expenses.
explanation: had (Simple Past - possession) → had had (Past Perfect)। sold (Simple Past) had sold (Past Perfect)। last year → the previous year। →

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: The professor said, "The students who had been absent yesterday will have to submit their assignments by tomorrow, or they will be penalized."
options: The professor said that the students who had been absent the day before would have to submit their assignments by the next day, or they would be penalized.
The professor said that the students who were absent yesterday would have to submit...
The professor said that the students who had been absent the day before will have to submit...
The professor said that the students who were absent the day before would have to submit...
explanation: 4 बड़े बदलाव: had been absent (Past Perfect नहीं बदलता), yesterday → the day before, will have to → would have to, will be penalized → would be penalized, और tomorrow → the next day। ऑप्शन में सभी बदलाव सटीक हैं।

set15.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: He told me, "I was trying to call you, but my phone's battery died, so I have been looking for a public phone since then."
options: He told me that he was trying to call you, but his phone's battery died...
He told me that he had been trying to call me, but his phone's battery died...
He told me that he had been trying to call me, but his phone's battery had died, so he had been looking for a public phone since then.
He told me that he had been trying... but his phone's battery died...
explanation: 3 Tense Shifts: was trying (Past Cont.) → had been trying, died (Simple Past) → had died, have been looking (Present Perf. Cont.) → had been looking। सिर्फ ऑप्शन (c) में तीनों को सही ढंग से बदला गया है।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: She asked him if he had been to the new cafe that had opened the previous week, and whether he thought it was better than the one they had been going to for months.
options: "Has you been to the new cafe...?" she asked him.
"Have you been to the new cafe that opened last week, and do you think it is better than the one we have been going to for months?" she asked him.
"Had you been to the new cafe..." she asked him.
"Did you go to the new cafe..." she asked him.
explanation: Indirect → Direct: had been → Have you been, had opened → opened (Simple Past), had been going → have been going। the previous week → last week।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The detective told the witnesses that they must tell him everything they had seen that morning, as it could be the key to solving the case that had been haunting the city for a long time.
options: "You must tell me everything you saw this morning, as it could be the key to solving the case that has been haunting the city for a long time," the detective said to the witnesses.
"You had to tell me everything you saw this morning..."
"You must tell me everything you had seen that morning..."
"You must tell him everything you saw this morning..."
explanation: they had seen → you saw, had been haunting → has been haunting, that morning → this morning। could be (Modal) नहीं बदलेगा।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The politician declared that he would not be swayed by the opposition's baseless accusations which were aimed at destabilizing the government that had been working for the welfare of the country for years.
options: "I will not be swayed... which were aimed... had been working..."
"I would not be swayed... which were aimed... was working..."

"I will not be swayed by the opposition's baseless accusations which are aimed at destabilizing the government that has been working for the welfare of the country for years," the politician declared.
"He would not be swayed... which were aimed... had been working..."
explanation: would not be → will not be, were aimed → are aimed, had been working → has been working। सभी पास्ट फॉर्म्स को वापस प्रेजेंट में लाना ज़रूरी है।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice: He said to her, "I must tell you that I was not the one who broke the vase, as I was not even in the room when it happened."
options: He told her that he had to tell her that he was not the one who had broken the vase...
He told her that he had to tell her that he had not been the one who had broken the vase, as he had not even been in the room when it had happened.
He told her that he must tell her that he was not the one...
He told her that he had to tell her that he was not the one...
explanation: must → had to, was not → had not been, broke → had broken, happened → had happened। (b) में सारे Simple Past को Past Perfect में बदला गया है।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The old woman explained with a sigh that she was feeling unwell and could not join the outing.
options: "I am unwell," she said, "and I cannot join the outing."
The old woman said, "I am unwell and I cannot join the outing."
She said, "I was unwell and I could not join the outing."
She explained, "I am unwell, I cannot join the outing."
explanation: was feeling unwell को am unwell और could not को cannot में बदला गया है।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The coach told the athletes that they had been training hard all season and deserved a break.
options: The coach said, "You were training hard all season and deserved a break."
The coach told the athletes, "You had been training hard all season and deserved a break."
The coach said to the athletes, "You have been training hard all season and deserve a break."
The coach told the athletes, "They had been training hard all season and deserved a break."
explanation: had been training → have been training और deserved → deserve। They वापस You बन जाएगा।

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: The young artist confidently announced that she could paint a masterpiece if she were given enough time.
options: The young artist announced, "I can paint a masterpiece if I am given enough time."
The young artist said, "She could paint a masterpiece if she was given enough time."
The young artist announced, "I could paint a masterpiece if I were given enough time."
The young artist said, "I can paint a masterpiece if I were given enough time."
explanation: could paint → can paint, if she were given → if I am given।

set16.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: He said that the machine operates automatically but had malfunctioned that morning.
options: He said, "The machine operated automatically but had malfunctioned that morning."
He said, "The machine operated automatically but had malfunctioned this morning."
He said, "The machine operates automatically, but malfunctioned this morning."
He said, "The machine is operating automatically, but malfunctioned this morning."
explanation: operates automatically (आदत/तथ्य) Indirect में भी नहीं बदला था, तो Direct में भी operates रहेगा। had malfunctioned → malfunctioned, that morning this morning। →

set16.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice: She said that by the next month, she would have been living in Paris for five years.
options: She said, "By next month, I will have been living in Paris for five years."
She said, "By next month, I would be living in Paris for five years."
She said, "By next month, I have been living in Paris for five years."
She said, "By the next month, I will live in Paris for five years."
explanation: would have been living will have been living (Future Perfect Continuous)। →

set16.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: The judge said to the prisoner, "What were you doing in the garage at 10:30 pm on Monday when the incident took place?"
options: The judge asked the prisoner what he was doing...
The judge asked the prisoner what he had been doing... when the incident took place.
The judge asked the prisoner what he had been doing in the garage at 10:30 pm on Monday when the incident had taken place.
The judge asked the prisoner what was doing...
explanation: were doing (Past Cont.) had been doing। took place (Simple Past) → had taken → place।

set17.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: The analyst remarked that the statistical data had evidently been misinterpreted, which might have skewed the report.
options: The analyst said, "The statistical data is evidently misinterpreted, which might have skewed the report."
The analyst said, "The statistical data had evidently been misinterpreted, which has skewed the report."
The analyst said, "The statistical data has evidently been misinterpreted, which may have skewed the report."
The analyst said, "The statistical data were evidently misinterpreted, which skewed the report."
explanation: had been → has been (Present Perfect), might have → may have।

set17.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: He told her that the documents had already been signed before she had called.
options: He said, "The documents were already signed before you called."
He said to her, "The documents have already been signed before you had called."
He said to her, "The documents had already been signed before you called."
He told her, "The documents have already been signed before you have called."
explanation: Past में दो एक्शन हों (Before लगा हो), तो Past Perfect (had already been signed) Direct में भी Past Perfect में ही रहता है। she had called → you called।

set17.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: She said to me, "We are going to live in a new city from next month onwards until my brother gets a new job."
options: She told me that they will be going to live...
She told me that they were going to live in a new city from the following month onwards until her brother got a new job.
She told me that she was going to live...
She told me that they were going to live... until my brother got...
explanation: are going → were going, next month → the following month, gets → got।

set17.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Suhani said to the clerk, "I phoned the officer to sign this document, but he just passed it to you."
options: Suhani told the clerk that she phoned the officer...
Suhani told the clerk that she had phoned the officer to sign that document, but he just passed it to him.
Suhani told the clerk that she had phoned the officer to sign that document, but he had just passed it to him.
Suhani told the clerk that she phoned the officer...
explanation: दो Simple Past (phoned, passed) हैं, दोनों Past Perfect (had phoned, had passed) में बदलेंगे। this → that।

set17.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Rajan said to Rini, "I have very often informed you that we have to attend the meeting every Saturday."
options: Rajan told Rini that he had very often informed her that they had to attend the meeting every Saturday.
Rajan told Rini that she must very often informed her...
Rajan told Rini that he had very often been informing her...
Rajan told Rini that he had very often informed her that they had been attending...
explanation: have informed → had informed, have to → had to। I/you → he/her।

set17.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Sohan said, "You will get up early tomorrow to go for a morning walk as you must qualify in the physical test."
options: Sohan said that he would get up early the day after to go for a morning walk as he had to qualify in the physical test.
Sohan said that he should get up early...
Sohan said that he should get up early... as he must qualify...
Sohan said that you would get up early...
explanation: will → would, tomorrow → the day after / the next day, must (obligation) had to। →

set17.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: He said that by the time she arrived, the guests had already started leaving.
options: He said, "By the time she has arrived, the guests were already leaving."
He said, "By the time she had arrived, the guests had already started leaving."
He said, "By the time she arrived, the guests had already started leaving."
He said, "By the time she arrived, the guests had already started to leave."
explanation: Time clause वाले जटिल वाक्यों में Tense का Sequence (Simple Past + Past Perfect) Direct Speech में भी वैसा ही रहता है।

set17.word: Choose the correct indirect narration: Sairam said, "She has to come to Delhi to me within the next couple of hours to take help to solve the problem she mentioned."
options: Sairam said that she had to come to Delhi to her within the following couple of hours...
Sairam said that she had to go to Delhi to him within the next couple of hours...
Sairam said that she has to go to Delhi...
Sairam said that she had to go to Delhi to him within the following couple of hours to take help to solve the problem she had mentioned.
explanation: has to come → had to go (Perspective change: 'आना' 'जाना' बन जाता है)। next → following, mentioned → had mentioned।

set17.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: She told the detective that she had been sleeping when the thieves had broken in.
options: She said to the detective, "I had slept when the thieves had broken in."
She said to the detective, "I had slept when the thieves had broken in."
She told the detective, "I had been sleeping when the thieves broke in."
She told the detective, "I had been asleep when the thieves were breaking in."
explanation: had been sleeping (Past Perfect Cont.) डायरे क्ट में भी ऐसा ही रह सकता है, और had broken in (Past Perfect) वापस broke in (Simple Past) में चला जाएगा।

set17.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: They asked whether the meeting was likely to begin soon or be delayed further.
options: They asked, "Was the meeting likely to begin soon or be delayed further?"
They asked, "Is the meeting likely to begin shortly or be delayed more?"
They asked, "Will the meeting likely begin soon or is it to be delayed further?"
They asked, "Is the meeting likely to begin soon or will it be delayed further?"
explanation: was likely → Is the meeting likely। 'or be delayed' → 'or will it be delayed' (Direct में दू सरे हिस्से का Modal स्पष्ट किया जाता है)।

set18.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: The mentor asked whether I had been preparing regularly and if I would be ready by Monday.
options: The mentor asked, "Have you been preparing regularly and will you be ready by Monday?"
The mentor asked, "Had you been preparing regularly and would you be ready by Monday?"
The mentor asked, "Were you preparing regularly and will you be ready by Monday?"
The mentor asked, "Have you prepared regularly and are you ready by Monday?"
explanation: had been preparing → Have you been preparing। would be → will you be।

set18.word: From the options given below, choose the correct indirect narration: Timothy said, "How nice the food in the restaurant was!"
options: Timothy exclaimed that the food in the restaurant was very nice.
Timothy exclaimed that the food in the restaurant had been very nice.
Timothy exclaimed that the food in the restaurant has been very nice.
Timothy exclaimed that the food in the restaurant was been very nice.
explanation: Exclamatory वाक्य है। How nice → very nice। was (Simple Past) → had been (Past Perfect)।

set18.word: Which among the given options best describes the indirect narration: Ravi said to Seema, "Who was polishing my shoes yesterday when I was asleep?"
options: Ravi asked Seema that who had been polishing his shoes the day before when he had been asleep.
Ravi asked Seema who had been polishing his shoes the day before when he had been asleep.
Ravi asked Seema who had been polishing his shoes yesterday when he had been asleep.
Ravi asked Seema who had been polishing his shoes the day before when he was asleep.
explanation: was polishing → had been polishing। was asleep → had been asleep। yesterday → the day before।

set18.word: From the alternatives given below, choose the correct indirect narration: Raghu said, "Meena! Why were you running in the field yesterday?"
options: Raghu said that Meena! Why you had been running in the field the other day.
Raghu asked Meena why she had been running in the field the day before.
Raghu asked Meena that she had been running in the field the day before.
Raghu asked Meena to be running in the field the day before.
explanation: "Meena!" Vocative case है, इसलिए asked Meena होगा। were running → had been running। yesterday → the day before।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the option that most accurately conveys it in indirect speech. "How strange that nobody noticed the missing file until today!" exclaimed the clerk.
options: The clerk exclaimed it was strange that nobody noticed the missing file until today.
The clerk exclaimed that it was strange that no one had noticed the missing file until that day.
The clerk said that it was strange no one had noticed file till now.
The clerk exclaimed how strange nobody had noticed the file.
explanation: How strange → it was strange। noticed (V2) → had noticed। today → that day।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: The supervisor asked whether the reports that were supposed to be finalized last week had been submitted to the review committee.
options: "Did you submit the reports meant to be finalized last week to the review committee?" the supervisor asked.
"Had the final reports from last week been submitted to the committee?" the supervisor asked.
"Have last week's reports been sent to the committee yet?" the supervisor asked.
"Were the reports due last week submitted to the review team?" the supervisor asked.
explanation: whether... had been submitted (Past Perfect Passive) को Direct Speech में भी Past Perfect Passive Interrogative (Had... been submitted?) के रूप में रखा गया है।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: The coordinator reminded the team that the deadline extension applied only to those who had submitted a formal request.
options: "Only those who submitted a formal request can use the deadline extension," the coordinator said.
"If you didn't formally request, the deadline extension doesn't apply," the coordinator reminded them.
"The extension is granted only to those who made formal requests," the coordinator said.
"Those who haven't requested cannot use the deadline extension," said the coordinator.
explanation: applied → is granted (Present)। had submitted → made (Simple Past)। ऑप्शन (c) सबसे सटीक भाव और टेन्स रिवर्सल दे रहा है।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: He told the volunteers that once the list was finalized, no further changes could be made under any condition.
options: "There will be no changes allowed after finalizing the list," he told the volunteers.
"Once we finalize the list, you must not make any changes," he said.
"No changes can be made once the list is finalized—under any condition," he said.
"Even after finalizing the list, changes might be accepted in rare cases," he said.
explanation: was finalized → is finalized। could be made → can be made।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the direct speech: The analyst observed that the report, while detailed, lacked consistency in its data interpretation.
options: "The report is detailed, but your data interpretation lacks consistency," the analyst observed.
"Though the report is detailed, the interpretation of data isn't consistent," said the analyst.
"It's a detailed report, but the way the data is interpreted is inconsistent," the analyst said.
"Your data interpretation lacks consistency despite the report being detailed," said the analyst.
explanation: while detailed → Though the report is detailed। lacked → isn't consistent / lacks।

set18.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the option that most accurately conveys it in indirect speech. "Could it be that we overlooked a critical variable during our preliminary analysis?" she speculated.
options: She speculated if it is could be that they had overlooked critical variable during their preliminary analysis.
She speculated whether they had overlooked a critical variable during their preliminary analysis.
She asked could it be they forgot a critical variable in analysis.
She speculated about the possibility of forgetting a variable critically.
explanation: "Could it be that we overlooked..." एक Speculative Yes/No Question है। इसे whether they had overlooked में बदला जाएगा (overlooked V2 had overlooked)। →

set19.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the option that most accurately conveys it in direct speech. The officer asserted that the operation could not have succeeded without local support.
options: "The operation could not have succeeded without local support," asserted the officer.
"The operation succeeded without local support," said the officer.
"Local support must have failed the operation," claimed the officer.
"Without support, the operation will not succeed," said the officer.
explanation: could not have succeeded (Past Perfect Modal) में कोई बदलाव नहीं होता। यह Direct Speech में भी वैसा ही रहता है।

set19.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in indirect speech: The officer said, "Had I known about the problem earlier, I would have helped."
options: The officer said he had helped if he had known it.
The officer told if he knew about the problem, he would help.
The officer said that he know if he had helped.
The officer said that if he had known about the problem earlier, he would have helped.
explanation: यह एक Type 3 Conditional (असंभव भूतकाल) वाक्य है (Had + V3 और would have + V3)।

set19.word: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence: Rashmi said to Reshma, "Mahima was dancing on the floor when she was called by her mother."
options: Rashmi told Reshma that Mahima had danced on the floor when she had been called by her mother.
Rashmi told Reshma that Mahima had been dancing on the floor when was called by her mother.
Rashmi told Reshma that Mahima had been dancing on the floor when she had been called by her mother.
Rashmi told Reshma that Mahima was dancing on the floor when she was called by her mother.
explanation: was dancing (Past Continuous) बदलकर had been dancing (Past Perfect Continuous) हो जाएगा। was called (Simple Past Passive) बदलकर had been called (Past Perfect Passive) हो जाएगा। दोनों पास्ट टेन्स का बैकशिफ्ट होना ज़रूरी है।

set19.word: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence: He told that when they had reached the station, the train had already left.
options: He said, "When we have reach the station, the train had already left."
He said, "When we reached the station, the train had already left."
He said, "When we were reaching the station, the train was already leaving."
He said, "When we reach the station, the train has already left."
explanation: Indirect में had reached (Past Perfect) और had already left (Past Perfect) है। Time Clause वाले वाक्यों में "When + Simple Past + Past Perfect" का कॉम्बिनेशन होता है (जब हम स्टेशन पहुँचे, ट्रेन जा चुकी थी)। इसलिए Direct Speech में reached और had already left सही कॉम्बिनेशन है ।

set19.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct Speech: She said that she could have passed the exam.
options: She said, "I would have passed the exam."
She said, "I might have passed the exam."
She said, "I should have passed the exam."
She said, "I could have passed the exam."
explanation: Modal Perfect structures जैसे could have, should have, would have आदि Direct से Indirect (और वापस) बदलते समय अपरिवर्तित (unchanged) रहते हैं। के वल Pronoun "she" वापस "I" बन जाएगा।

set19.word: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence: They requested to close the door if he was feeling cold.
options: He said, "Please closed the door if you are feeling cold."
He said, "Please close the door if you is feeling cold."
He said, "Please close the door if feel cold."
He said, "Please close the door if you are feeling cold."
explanation: requested वापस Direct Speech में Please बन जाएगा। was feeling (Past Continuous) वापस are feeling (Present Continuous) बन जाएगा।

set19.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. She said, "O that I were a bird!"
options: She said that she wished she were a bird.
She said that she has been a bird.
She said that she wanted to be a bird.
She said that she was a bird.
explanation: "O that!" एक गहरी काल्पनिक इच्छा (strong unreal wish) को दर्शाता है। इसलिए Reporting verb wished आएगा। चूँकि यह Subjunctive mood है, इसलिए "were" (Past Subjunctive) वैसा का वैसा ही रहेगा, यह 'was' या 'had been' में नहीं बदलेगा।

set19.word: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence. They said, "Please bring the basket here."
options: They requested to take the basket here.
They requested me to take the basket there.
They requested me to bring the basket there.
They requested me to bring the basket here.
