explanation: Please को requested में बदला जाता है। here बदलकर there हो जाएगा। "Bring" (पास लाना) संदर्भ के अनुसार "Take" (दू र ले जाना) में बदल जाता है क्योंकि Reporting करते समय वक्ता उस जगह पर नहीं है।

set19.word: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence. Rima said that if she had wings, she would fly in the sky.
options: Rima said, "If I had wings, I would fly in the sky."
Rima said, "If I had wings, I will fly in the sky."
Rima said, "If I have wings, I would fly in the sky."
Rima said, "If I have wings, I will flew in the sky."
explanation: यह एक Type 2 Conditional (काल्पनिक स्थिति) है। नियम: Narration में काल्पनिक स्थितियों (Unreal past / Subjunctive) के Tense में कोई बदलाव नहीं होता।

set19.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. She said, "I dare not oppose him."
options: She said she has not dared to oppose him.
She said she had dared not oppose him.
She said she dared not oppose him.
She said she dare not oppose him.
explanation: "Dare not" एक Semi-modal है। Indirect Speech में यह अपने Past form dared not में बदल जाता है।

set20.word: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence. He asked if I had been aware of the policies of the company.
options: He said, "Did you be aware of the policies of the company?"
He said, "Were you awared of the policies of the company?"
He said, "Was you aware of the policies of the company?"
He said, "Were you aware of the policies of the company?"
explanation: Indirect में had been aware (Past Perfect) है। Direct Speech में यह Were you aware (Simple Past) हो जाएगा। 'you' के साथ हमेशा 'were' लगता है। ('awared' कोई शब्द नहीं होता क्योंकि aware एक Adjective है)।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. She said to me, "Would you like some coffee?"
options: She asked that I would like some coffee.
She asked me if I would like some coffee.
She asked me would I like some coffee.
She offered me some coffee.
explanation: "Would you like some coffee?" वास्तव में एक प्रस्ताव (Offer) है, प्रश्न नहीं। इसलिए इसे सीधे offered Reporting verb का उपयोग करके बनाना सबसे सटीक और मानक (Standard) तरीका है।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. They said, "How foolish of him to say that!"
options: They exclaimed how foolish of him to say that.
They exclaimed that it had been very foolish of him to say that.
They exclaimed that he is very foolish to say that.
They said it was very foolish of him to say that.
explanation: यह एक Exclamatory वाक्य है । "How foolish" को Assertive बनाने के लिए "It was very foolish" माना जाता है। was (Simple Past) बैकशिफ्ट होकर had been (Past Perfect) बन जाएगा।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. He said, "It must be raining outside."
options: He said it should be raining outside.
He said it must be raining outside.
He said it might be raining outside.
He said it had to be raining outside.
explanation: जब must का प्रयोग किसी तार्कि क निष्कर्ष (logical deduction - पक्का ऐसा ही हो रहा होगा) के लिए होता है, तो Indirect Speech में यह नहीं बदलता है।

set20.word: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence. He exclaimed that the flower was looking very beautiful, which was placed in the vase.
options: He said, "How beautiful the flower is looking, which is placed in the vase!"
He said, "What beautiful the flower is looking which was placed in the vase!"
He said, "Which beautiful the flower was looking which is placed in the vase!"
He said, "The flower is looking very beautiful, which is placed in the vase!"
explanation: Indirect में very beautiful है । इसे Direct में बदलने के लिए How beautiful (विस्मयादिबोधक) का इस्तेमाल होगा। Past tense (was looking, was placed) वापस Present tense (is looking, is placed) में बदल जाएं गे।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct Speech. They said that they were supposed to arrive earlier.
options: They said, "We should arrive earlier."
They said, "We were supposed to arrive earlier."
They said, "We have to arrive earlier."
They said, "We are supposed to arrive earlier."
explanation: "were supposed to" एक Semi-modal है जो बीते हुए कल की उम्मीद (unfulfilled past obligation) दर्शाता है। यह Direct Speech में भी बिना Tense बदले वैसा ही रहेगा।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. She said, "How I wish I could meet him once!"
options: She hoped she would meet him.
She exclaimed that she could meet him once.
She said that she wished she could meet him once.
She asked that she meet him once.
explanation: "How I wish" एक काल्पनिक इच्छा को दर्शाता है। इसे Indirect में "she wished" में बदला जाता है। Modal could नहीं बदलेगा।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. He said, "May God bless you."
options: He told that God should bless me.
He said that God may bless me.
He wished God bless me.
He prayed that God might bless me.
explanation: "May God bless you" एक Optative sentence (प्रार्थना) है। Reporting verb prayed आएगा। May बैकशिफ्ट होकर might हो जाएगा और Subject (God) पहले आ जाएगा (God might)।

set20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct to Indirect Speech. The teacher said, "Let him try on his own."
options: The teacher said that he should let him try on his own.
The teacher ordered him to try on his own.
The teacher suggested that he try on his own.
The teacher said that let him try on his own.
explanation: जब "Let" का प्रयोग किसी सुझाव (suggestion) के लिए होता है, तो Reporting verb suggested आता है। इसके बाद 'that' clause में Base verb (Subjunctive mood: try बिना 's/es' के ) का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

set20.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the most accurate direct speech version: The librarian said that silence must be maintained in the library.
options: "Silence should be maintained in the library," said the librarian.
"You will maintain silence in the library," the librarian said.
"Maintain silence in the library," said the librarian.
"Must you maintain silence in the library?" asked the librarian.
explanation: Indirect में must be maintained एक कड़ा आदेश (obligation) दिखा रहा है। Direct speech में इसे एक साधारण Imperative Command "Maintain silence..." के रूप में सबसे अच्छे तरीके से व्यक्त किया जाता है।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the most accurate direct speech version: The manager told the staff that deadlines must be followed strictly.
options: "Deadlines must be followed strictly," said the manager.
"You should follow deadlines strictly," said the manager.
"Follow deadlines strictly," the manager said.
"Deadlines are to be followed," said the manager.
explanation: must जब पक्के नियम (strong obligation) को दिखाता है, तो यह Direct से Indirect में

set21.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the most accurate indirect speech version: I said, "He will be here by noon."
options: I said he will be there by noon.
I said that he would be here by noon.
I said that he would be there by noon.
I said he shall be there by noon.
explanation: will → would और here there में बदल जाएगा। →

set21.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Choose the most accurate direct speech version: She said that she used to play the violin when she was young.
options: "I played the violin when I was young," she said.
"I use to play the violin when I was young," she said.
"I used to play the violin when I was young," she said.
"I was playing the violin when I was young," she said.
explanation: "Used to" (Past Habit दिखाने वाला Semi-modal) Direct Speech में नहीं बदलता है, यह used to ही रहता है।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the most accurate indirect speech version: The scientist said, "We have been working on this project for months."
options: The scientist said they had been working on that project for months.
The scientist said they have been working on this project for months.
The scientist said that they were working on that project.
The scientist said that we had worked on this project.
explanation: have been working (Present Perfect Cont.) → had been working (Past Perfect Cont.)। this → that। We → they।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the most accurate indirect speech version: She said, "Let him not be disturbed during the meeting."
options: She asked not to disturb him during the meeting.
She advised that he should not be disturbed during the meeting.
She said that let him not be disturbed.
She ordered no one disturb him.
explanation: "Let him not be disturbed" एक सलाह/सुझाव है। Indirect में इसे advised that he should not be disturbed में बदला जाता है।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the most accurate indirect speech version: The patient said, "It has been three days since I last slept."
options: The patient said that it was three days since he last slept.
The patient said that it had been three days since he had last slept.
The patient said that he didn't sleep for three days.
The patient said that he was not sleeping for three days.
explanation: has been (Present Perfect) → had been (Past Perfect)। since के बाद आने वाला slept (Simple Past) → had slept (Past Perfect) में बदल जाएगा।

set21.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The physicist remarked that time-reversal invariance could be violated under non-unitary evolution.
options: The physicist said, "Time-reversal invariance can be violated under non-unitary evolution."
He remarked, "If evolution is non-unitary, time-reversal invariance will be violated."
The physicist said, "Time reversal invariance could be violated under non-unitary evolution."
He said, "Time reversal violates when evolution non unitary,"
explanation: Indirect में Modal could है , जो Direct Speech में वापस can में बदल जाता है (Present Ability/Possibility)।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. Choose the most accurate indirect speech version: I said, "Let us start early."
options: I said let us start early.
I told that we must start early.
I suggested that we should start early.
I suggested us to start early.
explanation: "Let us" प्रस्ताव (proposal) दर्शाता है। Reporting verb suggested होगा और इसे that we should + V1 से जोड़ा जाएगा।

set21.word: Select the correct option for the Direct Speech conversion: The curator noted that the pigments were deteriorating faster than the varnish could protect them.
options: The curator noted, "The pigments are deteriorating faster than the varnish can protect them."
The curator noted, "The pigments were deteriorating faster than the varnish can be protected by them."
The curator noted, "Pigments deteriorate faster than varnish protects."
The curator noted, "Pigment deterioration faster than varnish can protect."
explanation: were deteriorating → are deteriorating। could protect → can protect। दोनों Tenses को वापस Present में लाया गया है।

set21.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech: The director noted that the figures had been inflated.
options: The director said, "These figures have been inflated."
The director said, "The figures are inflated."
The director said, "The figures were exaggerated."
The director said, "They inflated the figure"
explanation: had been inflated (Past Perfect Passive) वापस have been inflated (Present Perfect Passive) में आ जाएगा।

set22.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech: She asked him if he had ever been to the Taj Mahal.
options: She asked him, "Did you ever go to the Taj Mahal?"
She asked him, "Were you at the Taj Mahal?"
She asked him, "Have you ever been to the Taj Mahal?"
She asked him, "Do you ever go to the Taj Mahal?"
explanation: Indirect में had ever been (Past Perfect) है। Direct में यह Have you ever been (Present Perfect Interrogative) बन जाएगा।

set22.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion into Direct Speech: He said that while he had been writing the report, his laptop suddenly shut down.
options: He said, "While I wrote the report, my laptop suddenly shut down."
He said, "While I was writing the report, my laptop suddenly shut down."
He said, "While I am writing the report, my laptop suddenly shuts down."
He said, "While I had been writing the report, my laptop had shut down."
explanation: had been writing (Past Perfect Cont.) वापस was writing (Past Cont.) हो जाएगा। shut down (Simple Past) जस का तस रहेगा क्योंकि यह 'Past Continuous' के बीच में होने वाली अचानक घटना (Interrupting action) है।

set22.word: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech: She said that she would arrive before noon.
options: She said, "I shall arrive before noon."
She said, "I will have arrived before noon."
She said, "I would arrive before noon."
She said, "I had arrived before noon."
explanation: Indirect में would arrive है । Direct में Pronoun I के साथ (British English के नियमों के अनुसार) Future Tense के लिए shall का प्रयोग सबसे सटीक माना जाता है, इसलिए I shall arrive सही होगा।

set22.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding indirect speech: She said, "If only I hadn't trusted him!"
options: She had regret that she had trusted him.
She said that she wished she hadn't trusted him.
She said if only she hadn't trusted him.
She wished she didn't trust him.
explanation: "If only..." एक गहरी इच्छा या पछतावे (regretful wish) को दर्शाता है। इसे she wished में बदला जाता है। hadn't trusted (Past Perfect) का Tense नहीं बदलता।

set22.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding indirect speech: She said, "I was going to the market."
options: She said that she had been going to the market.
She said she was gone to the market.
She said she is going to the market.
She said she had gone to the market.
explanation: was going (Past Continuous) → had been going (Past Perfect Continuous)। I she। →

set22.word: A sentence is provided in direct speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding indirect speech: He said, "I wish I had not wasted those years."
options: He said he wished he had not wasted those years.
He said he wished not to waste those years.
He said that he did not waste those years.
He wished that he would not waste those years.
explanation: "I wish" → he wished (Simple Past)। had not wasted (Past Perfect) अपरिवर्तित (unchanged) रहेगा क्योंकि यह पहले से ही अधिकतम पास्ट में है।

set22.word: Choose the correct direct sentence of the following sentence: He said that they had not been given any clear instructions.
options: He said, "They are not given any clear instructions."
He said, "They were not given any clear instructions."
He said, "They had not been given any clear instructions."
He said, "They haven't been given any clear instructions."
explanation: Indirect में had not been given (Past Perfect Passive) है। यदि वक्ता ने मूल रूप से भी 'Past Perfect' का इस्तेमाल किया था (किसी अन्य घटना से पहले का काम), तो Direct Speech में भी यह had not been given ही रहेगा।

set22.word: Choose the correct indirect speech of the sentence: He said, "Whoever did this must be held accountable."
options: He said that whoever did it must be held to account.
He said that the one who did this must be accounted.
He said that whoever had done that must be held accountable.
He said that whoever did this must have held accountable.
explanation: did (Simple Past) → had done (Past Perfect)। this → that। must (Obligation) अपरिवर्तित रहेगा।

set22.word: Choose the correct indirect speech of the sentence: The general said, "May our nation always remain free."
options: The general said our nation may always remain free.
The general expressed the wish that their nation might always remain free.
The general hoped that his nation will remain free.
The general wished the nation would stay free.
explanation: "May... remain" एक इच्छा (wish) है। expressed the wish that का प्रयोग होगा। our → their। May → might।

set22.word: Choose the correct direct sentence of the following sentence: He said that the committee had decided to postpone the event.
options: He said, "The committee has decided to postpone the event."
He said, "The committee had decided to postpone the event."
He said, "The event was postponed by the committee."
He said, "The committee would postpone the event."
explanation: यहाँ रिपोर्ट की गई घटना पहले ही पूर्ण (past perfect) थी, इसलिए Direct Speech में भी had decided ही रहेगा।

set23.word: Choose the correct indirect narration from the given options for the following direct narration: Mina said, "Do not touch the cable when the indicator is blinking red."
options: Mina said that do not touch the cable when the indicator has been blinking red.
Mina said that not to touch the cable when the indicator was blinking red.
Mina forbade to touch the cable when the indicator was blinking red.
Mina forbidden to touch the cable when the indicator was blinking red.
explanation: Do not touch (Negative Imperative) को forbade to touch (मना किया) में बदला जाता है। is blinking → was blinking।

set23.word: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion of the following sentence into Direct Speech. She asked whether it hadn't been an extremely challenging puzzle.
options: She said, "Isn't it an extremely challenging puzzle?"
She asked, "Hadn't it an extremely challenging puzzle?"
She asked, "Wasn't it an extremely challenging puzzle?"
She said, "Hadn't it been an extremely challenging puzzle!"
explanation: Indirect में whether it hadn't been (Past Perfect Negative) है। Direct में यह Wasn't it (Simple Past Negative Interrogative) में वापस आ जाएगा।

set23.word: Convert the indirect sentence to direct speech. The child exclaimed with joy that he had won the race.
options: The child said, "I have won the race!"
The child exclaimed, "I have won the race!"
The child said, "Hurrah! I have won the race!"
The child shouted, "Winning the race!"
explanation: Indirect में had won है जो Direct में have won हो जाएगा। "exclaimed with joy" का भाव दर्शाने के लिए Reporting Verb exclaimed और Exclamation mark (!) का उपयोग किया गया है। (अक्सर "Hurrah!" जोड़ा जाता है, लेकिन अगर ऑप्शन में सिर्फ "exclaimed" दिया हो, तो वह भी व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है)।

set23.word: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence. Nandini said to Minal, "What shall I give you on your birthday next year?"
options: Nandini asked Minal what she would give her on her birthday the following year.
Nandini asked Minal what she would give her on her birthday the previous year.
Nandini asked Minal what she should give her on her birthday next year.
Nandini requested Minal what she should give her on her birthday the coming year.
explanation: shall (Future) → would। I/you → she/her। next year → the following year। ऑप्शन (a) सभी बैकशिफ्ट नियमों का सही ढंग से पालन करता है ।

set24.word: My friend requested me to bring him a sandwich.
options: He said, "My friend, please bring me a sandwich."
My friend said, "Will you bring me a sandwich."
"Please bring me a sandwich", said my friend.
"Please bring my friend a sandwich", said he.
explanation: Sentence indirect speech mein hai aur usme "requested" word use hua hai. Jab bhi indirect mein 'request' hota hai, toh uski direct speech hamesha "Please" ya "Kindly" se shuru hoti hai. Option 3 mein "Please" bhi hai aur speaker "my friend" bhi sahi jagah par hai.

set24.word: She said to the children, "You must do the work".
options: She told the children that they had to do the work.
She told the children that they must do the work.
She told the children to do the work.
She told the children that they have to do the work.
explanation: Jab "must" ka use kisi majboori (compulsion) ya niyam ko dikhane ke liye hota hai past tense mein, toh indirect banate waqt "must" badal kar "had to" ban jata hai. Yahan "said to" banega "told", "You" (2nd person) bacchon (children) ke hisab se "they" banega, aur "must" -> "had to".

options: Everybody says that she sings well.
Everybody says how well she sings.
Everybody tells that she sang very well.
Everybody says that she sang well.
explanation: Yahan bahar ka reporting verb "says" (Present Tense) hai. Golden rule yaad karo: bahar Present ho toh andar ka Tense change nahi hoga! Exclamatory sentence ("How well she sings!") ko simple assertive sentence mein badalte hain: "She sings well". Isliye Option 1 perfect hai, tense bhi change nahi hua.

set24.word: Daman says, "Priya is working in Ahmedabad."
options: Daman says that Priya was working in Ahmedabad.
Daman said that Priya was working in Ahmedabad.
Daman said that Priya works in Ahmedabad.
Daman says that Priya is working in Ahmedabad.
explanation: Same Q3 wala rule! Bahar "says" (Present Tense) hai. Toh andar wale sentence ("is working") ka tense change nahi hoga, wo "is working" hi rahega. Bas comma hata kar "that" laga do.

set24.word: The old man said that he would soon be leaving the world.
options: The old man said, "I shall soon be leaving the world."
The old man said, "I am soon leaving the world."
The old man said, "I would soon leave the world."
The old man said, "I am going to leave the world soon."
explanation: Yeh indirect speech hai. Andar "would be leaving" hai. Hum jante hain ki direct speech mein 'I' ya 'We' ke sath 'shall/will' lagta hai jo indirect mein subject ke hisab se 'would' ban jata hai. Toh direct speech mein "I shall/will soon be leaving" hoga. Option 1 exactly isko match karta hai.

set24.word: "Come home early" said Mr. Matthew to his clerk.
options: Mr Matthew told his clerk to come home as early as possible.
Mr Matthew asked his clerk to be home early.
Mr Matthew ordered his clerk to come home early.
Mr Matthew requested his clerk to come home a little early.
explanation: Yeh ek Imperative (aadesh wale) sentence hai. Ek boss (Mr. Matthew) apne clerk ko request nahi karta, usually aadesh (order) deta hai. Aise sentences mein verbs ko 'to' laga kar jodte hain (to + V1). So, "ordered his clerk to come...".

set24.word: I said, "Water is essential for life."
options: Water is essential for life was said by me.
I told that water was essential for life.
I told that water was essential for life. (Options 2 & 3 are same)
I said that water is essential for life.
explanation: Rule: Agar reported speech koi Universal Truth, Fact, ya Proverb (kahawat) hai, toh bahar past tense (said) hone ke bawajood andar ka tense change nahi hota. Paani life ke liye essential kal bhi tha, aaj bhi hai. Toh "is" ka "was" nahi hoga. Option 4 sahi hai.

set24.word: She asked me if I was going to college.
options: She said, "Am I going to college?"
She said to me, "Are you going to college?"
She asked me, "Will you go to college?"
She asked to me, "Was I going to college?"
explanation: Yeh ek Interrogative (sawaal wala) indirect sentence hai jisme "if" laga hai. Iska matlab direct speech ek Helping Verb (is/are/am) se shuru hui hogi. "I was going" ka active hoga "Are you going?" kyunki jab "She said to me" hota hai, toh "you" mujh (me) ke hisab se "I" ban jata hai.

set24.word: Iba said that she might go home the next day with her sister.
options: Iba said, "I could go home tomorrow with my sister."
Iba said, "I may go home tomorrow with my sister."
Iba said, "I can go home tomorrow with my sister."
Iba said, "I will go home tomorrow with my sister."
explanation: Indirect mein "might" hai, jo direct mein pakka "may" raha hoga. "the next day" direct mein "tomorrow" hota hai. "she" direct mein Iba khud ke liye "I" bolegi. In sabhi conditions ko Option 2 fulfill karta hai.

options: He said that he had been doing it.
He said that he had done it.
He said that he has done it.
He said that he did it.
explanation: "was doing" (Past Continuous Tense) hai. Rule ke hisab se Past Continuous hamesha Past Perfect Continuous (had been + V1+ing) mein change hota hai. Toh "was doing" badal kar "had been doing" ho jayega.

set25.word: The lady asked me how my uncle was.
options: The lady said to me, "How is your uncle?"
The lady asked me, "How has your uncle been?"
The lady said to me, "How was your uncle?"
The lady asked me, "How is your uncle doing?"
explanation: Indirect speech "Wh-word" (how) se shuru ho rahi hai. "my uncle was" past mein hai, yani direct mein yeh present ("is") mein hoga. Interrogative ka format hota hai: Wh-word + Helping Verb + Subject. Toh "How is your uncle?" direct speech banegi.

set25.word: He said, "Honesty is the best policy."
options: He said that honesty is the best policy.
He said that honesty was the best policy.
He said that honesty had been the best policy.
He said that honesty has been the best policy.
explanation: Wahi Universal Fact/Kahawat wala rule! "Imaandari sabse acchi neeti hai" ek universal truth hai. Isliye bahar past tense ('said') hone par bhi iska tense change nahi hoga. 'is' ka 'was' nahi banega.

set25.word: He said that he intended to leave for Surat that night.
options: "I intend to leave for Surat that night" he said.
"I intend to left for Surat that night" he said.
"I intended to leave for Surat tonight" he said.
"I intend to leave for Surat tonight" he said.
explanation: Indirect mein "intended" (V2) hai, toh direct mein "intend" (V1) raha hoga (Kyunki Present Indefinite -> Past Indefinite banta hai). Doosra clue: "that night" indirect mein hai, toh direct mein yeh "tonight" raha hoga. Option 4 dono check boxes tick karta hai.

set25.word: The prisoner enquired if a lawyer had been arranged for him.
options: The prisoner enquired, "Is there a lawyer for me?"
The prisoner asked, "Has a lawyer been arranged for me?"
The prisoner enquired, "Had a lawyer been arranged for me?"
The prisoner said, "Have you arranged a lawyer for me?"
explanation: Indirect mein "had been arranged" (Past Perfect) hai. Direct mein yeh Present Perfect ("has/have been arranged") raha hoga. Sawaal hai isliye "Has a lawyer been arranged..." se shuru hoga.

options: You will say that you are ill.
You would say that you were ill.
You would say that you are ill.
You will say that you were ill.
explanation: Bahar reporting verb "will say" (Future Tense) hai. Golden rule: bahar Present/Future ho toh andar ka tense change nahi hota! "I am ill" ka "you are ill" ho jayega (kyunki "I" subject "You" ke hisab se change hoga). Par tense present hi rahega.

options: "I will see you later" he told his friends.
"I am bidding you Goodbye."
He said, "Goodbye, my friends."
"Goodbye, my friends" he was saying to them.
explanation: Jab bhi hum kisi ko alvida (goodbye/farewell) kehte hain, toh 'say' badal kar 'bade' (bid ka past tense) ho jata hai. Direct speech mein clearly "Goodbye, my friends" hoga, tabhi indirect mein "He bade his friends goodbye" bana.

set25.word: He exclaimed with joy that their team had won the tournament.
options: He said, "Our team has won the tournament"
He exclaims, "Wow! Our team won the tournament"
He exclaimed, "Hurrah! Our team has won the tournament!"
He said, "Our team won the tournament."
explanation: Indirect mein "exclaimed with joy" hai. Iska direct roop ek Exclamatory word hoga jaise "Hurrah!" ya "Wow!". Yahan "had won" hai (Past Perfect), jo direct mein Present Perfect ("has won") raha hoga. Option 3 bilkul perfect hai.

set25.word: His father said that it was time he visited his uncle.
options: "It was time you visited your uncle" said his father.
"It is time you visited my uncle" said his father.
"It is time you visit your uncle" said his father.
"It is time you visited your uncle" said his father.
explanation: English grammar mein phrase "It is time" ya "It is high time" ke baad hamesha Past Indefinite (V2) ka use hota hai (unreal past). "It is time you visited". Jab isko indirect banate hain, toh 'is' badal kar 'was' ban jata hai, par 'visited' change nahi hota kyunki wo ek fixed structure hai. Isliye direct form Option 4 hai.

set25.word: He said, "I had a wonderful dream last night".
options: He said that he saw a wonderful dream last night.
He said that he had seen a wonderful dream yesterday.
He said that last night he had a wonderful dream.
He said that he had had a wonderful dream the previous night.
explanation: Dhyan dena, yahan "had" ek main verb (V2) hai past indefinite ki. Past Indefinite hamesha Past Perfect (had + V3) mein change hota hai. Toh helping verb aayegi 'had', aur V3 bhi aayegi 'had' (have ka past participle). Isliye "had had" banega. "Last night" change hoga "the previous night" mein. Option 4 is absolutely correct.

set25.word: He said, "I am buying a new pen".
options: He said that he bought a new pen.
He said that he was buying a new pen.
He said that he had been buying a new pen.
He said that he is buying a new pen.
explanation: "am buying" (Present Continuous) seedha-seedha Past Continuous mein change hoga -> "was buying". Subject 'I', 'He' ban jayega.

set26.word: "You did not return your book yesterday," said the librarian.
options: The librarian said that he had not returned his book the previous day.
The librarian said that he had not returned his book yesterday.
The librarian said the book not been returned the previous day.
The librarian said that you had not returned your book yesterday.
explanation: "did not return" (Past Indefinite). Change hoga Past Perfect mein: "had not returned". "yesterday" badlega "the previous day" mein. 'You' aur 'your' badlenge kisi third person ('he', 'his') mein.

set26.word: "You are all doing very well," said Mr. Jones.
options: Mr. Jones said that we are all doing well.
Mr. Jones said that they were all doing well.
Mr. Jones said that they were all doing very well.
Mr. Jones said that you are all doing very well.
explanation: "are doing" (Present Continuous) badlega Past Continuous mein "were doing". 'You all' yahan 'they all' ya 'we all' ban sakta hai, par option 3 aur 2 mein difference sirf 'very' word ka hai. Question mein 'very well' hai, isliye answer mein bhi 'very well' zaroori hai.

set26.word: "I finished it several days ago," said Jack.
options: Jack said that he finished it several days previously.
Jack said that he had finished it several days earlier.
Jack said that he finished it several days earlier.
Jack said that he finish it several days ago.
explanation: "finished" (Past Indefinite, V2). Change hoga Past Perfect (had + V3) mein -> "had finished". Aur time word "ago" hamesha "earlier" ya "before" mein change hota hai. Option 2 ekdum correct hai.

set26.word: "I gave the packet to Tom, who kept it a long time," said Pamela.
options: Pamela said that she had gave the packet to Tom, who kept it a long time.
Pamela said that she had given the packet to Tom, to keep for a long time.
Pamela said that she had given the packet to Tom, who kept it a long time.
Pamela said that she had given the packet to Tom, who kept it for long time.
explanation: "gave" (V2) change hoga "had given" (Past Perfect) mein. Baad wale clause ("who kept...") ka tense sometimes change nahi karte agar wo ek relative time frame ko complex bana raha ho ya padhne mein odd lage. Rule ke hisaab se "who had kept" aana chahiye tha, par diye gaye 4 options mein se Option 3 hi gramatically correct structures follow kar raha hai. (Option 1 mein 'had gave' hai jo galat hai).

set26.word: "I will put this key here." said the caretaker.
options: The caretaker says that he would put the key there.
The caretaker said that he will put the key there.
The caretaker said that he would put the key there.
The caretaker says that he would put the key here.
explanation: "will" ka banega "would". "this" ka banega "that" (ya the). "here" ka banega "there". Toh sentence hoga: The caretaker said that he would put the/that key there.

options: He said that he would go the next day.
He said that he shall go the next day.
He said that he should go tomorrow.
He said that he would go tomorrow.
explanation: 'shall' hamesha 'should' nahi banta. Jab subject 'I' se badal kar 'he' banta hai indirect speech mein, toh future ke hisab se he ke sath 'would' lagana grammatically correct mana jata hai. "tomorrow" badal kar "the next day" ho jayega.

set26.word: Walter said, "When I was a child, I wasn't afraid of ghosts".
options: Walter said that when he had been a child, he wasn't afraid of ghosts.
Walter said that when he was a child, he wasn't afraid of ghosts.
Walter says that he wasn't afraid when he was a child.
Walter said that when he was a child, he hadn't been afraid of ghosts.
explanation: Ek khaas Exception (Apwaad) yaad rakhna: Jab kisi Time Clause (jo 'when' ya 'while' se shuru ho) mein Past Tense hota hai aur sath mein dusra past action hota hai, toh indirect banate waqt Tense change nahi kiya jata. Isliye "was a child" aur "wasn't afraid" as it is rahenge, bas pronoun 'I' ka 'he' banega.

set26.word: The master said that he would see me the next day.
options: "You will be seen by me tomorrow," said the master.
"I will see you tomorrow," said the master.
"Tomorrow, I will see you," says the master.
"I will be seeing you tomorrow," said the master.
explanation: Indirect mein "would see" hai, yani direct mein "will see" / "shall see" hoga. "the next day" hai, yani direct mein "tomorrow" hoga. Option 2 bilkul seedha aur sahi hai: "I will see you tomorrow".

set26.word: Father told Peter to clean his shoes.
options: "Clean your shoes, Peter," says father.
"Clean your shoes, Peter," told father.
"Clean your shoes, Peter," asked father.
"Clean your shoes, Peter," said father.
explanation: Yeh Imperative sentence ka indirect hai ("to clean"). Direct mein hamesha "said" ya "said to" ka use hota hai. (Told ya asked indirect verbs hain). Option 4 mein "said father" ka use kiya gaya hai aur sentence direct command de raha hai.

set26.word: The girl said, "How happy I am!"
options: The girl exclaimed that she is very happy.
The girl said how happy she was.
The girl exclaimed that how happy she was.
The girl exclaimed that she was very happy.
explanation: "How happy I am!" ek Exclamatory sentence hai. Indirect mein isko simple Assertive sentence ("I am very happy") banaya jata hai. Phir tense change karte hain "am" -> "was". 'I' ban jayega 'she'. "The girl exclaimed that she was very happy."

set27.word: Mohan says that the teacher is not at home.
options: Mohan said, "Teacher is not at home."
Mohan said, "Teacher was not at home."
Mohan says, "Teacher is not at home."
Mohan says, "Teacher was not at home."
explanation: Bahar reporting verb Present tense ("says") mein hai. Indirect mein "says that" aaya hai toh direct mein bhi "says" hi aayega (Tense ka koi panga nahi hoga dono taraf). Option 3 mein "says" hai aur andar ka tense present hi rakha hai.

set27.word: The student said, "I am doing my homework."
options: The student said that she was doing her homework.
The student said that she did her homework.
The student said that she has been doing her homework.
The student said that she is doing her homework.
explanation: "am doing" (Present Continuous). Isko Past Continuous "was doing" mein badal do. 'I' student ke hisab se 'he/she' banega aur 'my' 'his/her' banega. Option 1 follow kar raha hai.

set27.word: I said to the boy, "Get out before you are driven out."
options: I told the boy get out before you are driven out.
I told the boy to get out before he is driven out.
I told the boy to get out before he was driven out.
I ordered the boy to get out before he was driven out.
explanation: "Get out" ek strong command (order) hai. Toh 'said to' ban jayega 'ordered'. Dono verbs ko infinitive "to get out" se jodenge. Aage wale hisse "you are driven out" (Present) ko bhi past mein change karenge "he was driven out".

set27.word: Amelia is going to ask the principal for permission to go on a study tour.
options: Amelia is going to say to the principal, "Might we have your permission to go on a study tour?"
Amelia is going to say to the principal, "Would we have your permission to go on a study tour?"
Amelia is going to say to the principal, "Should we have your permission to go on a study tour?"
Amelia is going to say to the principal, "May we have your permission to go on a study tour?"
explanation: Principal se 'permission' lene ke liye English mein hamesha modal verb "May" ka use sabse formal aur correct mana jata hai ("May I come in?"). Isliye option 4 ("May we have...") ekdum theek hai.

set27.word: Janet said that she had lost all her belongings at the airport that morning.
options: Janet said, "I had to lose all my belongings at the airport this morning."
Janet said, "I lost all my belongings at the airport this morning."
Janet said, "I lost all my belongings at the airport that morning."
Janet said, "I had lost all my belongings at the airport that morning."
explanation: Indirect mein "had lost" (Past Perfect) hai. Rule kehta hai ki Past Indefinite (V2) badal kar Past Perfect banta hai. Toh direct mein V2 ("lost") aayega. Aur "that morning" time phrase hai jo direct speech mein "this morning" raha hoga. Option 2 correct rules use kar raha hai.

set27.word: She said to herself, "Shall I have a happy married life?"
options: She wondered if she would have a happy married life.
She asked that if she should have a happy married life.
She thought that she should have a happy married life.
She wondered if she shall have a happy married life.
explanation: Yeh ek Interrogative (sawaal) sentence hai jahan wo khud se ek doubt/wonder express kar rahi hai. Isliye "said to herself" badal kar "wondered" ho jayega. "Shall I" past mein change hoga. 'I' banega 'she'. Aur 'she' ke sath future ki past form 'would' use hoti hai ('should' nahi, kyunki 'should' ka matlab duty ya advice ho jata hai). Isliye Option 1 sabse perfect sense banata hai.

set27.word: I said to you, "You should believe her".
options: I told you that I should believe her.
I told you that you should believe her.
I told to you that you should believe her.
I said to you that you should believe her.
explanation: "said to" ka indirect mein "told" banta hai (Option 4 reject). "told" ke baad "to" nahi lagta (Option 3 reject). Ab andar chalte hain: "You" (2nd person) humesha Object (bahar wale "you") ke hisab se badalta hai. Toh 'you' ka 'you' hi rahega. 'should' ek modal hai jo indirect mein change nahi hota. Isliye Option 2 bilkul theek hai.

set27.word: "Oh, how could I ever thank you, Sir!" said the convict to the Bishop.
options: The convict exclaimed gratefully to the Bishop that how he could ever thank him.
Exclaiming gratefully, the convict said to the Bishop that he could never thank him enough.
The convict said to the Bishop that he could never thank him enough.
The convict told the Bishop that it was difficult for him to thank him.
explanation: Sentence ek strong emotion (gratitude/ehsaan) dikha raha hai. "Oh, how could I ever thank you" ek rhetorical question hai jiska indirect matlab hai "I can never thank you enough". "Sir" word batata hai ki respect/gratitude hai. Isliye "Exclaiming gratefully" wala structure (Option 2) us feeling ko best capture karta hai, baaki options sirf literal translation hain jo emotion miss kar dete hain.

set27.word: The young man said to his father, "Pardon me, sir."
options: The young man begged of his father to pardon him.
The young man told his father pardon me.
The young man requested to his father pardon me, sir.
The young man begged of his father to pardon me.
explanation: "Pardon me" (Mujhe maaf kar do) ek maafi ya request hai. Jab koi father ya elder se aisi request karta hai, toh "begged" (bheekh mangna ya highly request karna) use hota hai. "Pardon" ek verb hai jisko infinitive (to + verb) bana kar jodte hain: "to pardon". 'me' badlega young man ke hisaab se 'him'. Isliye Option 1 correct hai.

set27.word: He said, "Oh, how I would have loved to visit Kashmir this year."
options: He exclaimed how I would have loved to visit Kashmir this year.
He exclaimed that how he would have loved to visit Kashmir that year.
He said that he will have loved to visit Kashmir this year.
He exclaimed that he would have really loved to visit Kashmir that year.
explanation: "Oh, how I would have loved..." ek exclamatory sentence hai jo ek strong ichha (wish) dikhata hai. Indirect mein "how" hatakar ek simple assertive sentence banate hain jisme uski intensity dikhane ke liye "really/very" add karte hain. Tense "would have loved" same rahega. "this year" time word hai jo "that year" banega. Option 4 inn sabko sahi tarike se follow karta hai.

set28.word: My friend said, "Hello! What are you doing here?"
options: My friend said hello to me and asked what were you doing here.
My friend said hello and asked me what I was doing there.
My friend greeted me and asked me what I was doing there.
My friend wished me and asked me what was I doing there.
explanation: "Hello!" ek greeting hai, isliye "said" ko "greeted" mein badla jayega. Dusra part question ("What are you doing here?") hai. Isko jodte waqt hum "asked" lagayenge. "are doing" (Present) past mein "was doing" banega. Aur Interrogative ka format (HV + Sub) indirect mein Assertive (Sub + HV) ho jata hai -> "what I was doing". "here" banega "there". Option 3 sab rules perfectly manta hai.

set28.word: "Don't be late tomorrow morning," my mother said to me.
options: My mother warned me not to be late the next day morning.
My mother asks me not to be late tomorrow morning.
My mother told me don't be late the next morning.
My mother warned me to not be late the next day morning.
explanation: "Don't" wale imperative sentences ka indirect "not to" + V1 se banta hai (Option 4 mein "to not be" hai jo split infinitive ka error hai, halanki bolchal mein chalta hai par grammar mein "not to be" better hai). "tomorrow" banega "the next day". Mother warn/advise kar sakti hai. Option 1 grammatically sabse theek hai.

set28.word: "Remove all the stains carefully and clean the windows," said the contractor to the worker.
options: The contractor requested the worker to remove all the stains carefully and clean the windows.
The contractor instructed the worker to remove all the stains carefully and clean the windows.
The contractor said to the worker that you should remove all the stains carefully and clean the windows.
The contractor told the worker that by removing all the stains carefully, the windows will be cleaned.
explanation: Ek contractor apne worker ko order ya instruction deta hai, request nahi karta. Yeh imperative sentence hai, toh dono clauses ko seedha infinitive "to" laga kar jodenge: "instructed... to remove...". Option 2 best meaning and structure deliver karta hai.

set28.word: You told me that you had not slept the previous night and could not work then.
options: You said to me, "I have not slept last night and could not work then."
You said to me, "You did not sleep the previous night and could not work now."
You said to me, "I have not slept the previous night and cannot work then."
You said to me, "I did not sleep last night and cannot work now."
explanation: Yeh Indirect hai. "had not slept" (Past Perfect) hai, yani direct mein Past Indefinite (did not sleep) raha hoga. "the previous night" ka direct mein "last night" hoga. Aur "could not work then" mein "could" (Past) aur "then" (Time) direct mein respectively "can" aur "now" rahe honge. Option 4 sari conditions poori karta hai.

set28.word: The teacher asked me why I had reached the examination hall so late.
options: The teacher said to me, "Why are you reaching the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why you reached the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why had I reached the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why did you reach the examination hall so late?"
explanation: Indirect mein "had reached" (Past Perfect) hai. Direct mein kya hoga? Past Indefinite! Sawaal 'Why' se shuru hai. Past Indefinite ka interrogative form hota hai: Wh-word + did + Sub + V1. "Why did you reach..." bilkul perfect direct speech hai.

set28.word: I told you that I had bought a new book for you the day before.
options: I said to you, "I bought a new book for you yesterday."
I said to you, "You have bought a new book for me the day before."
I told to you, "I have bought a new book for you yesterday."
I said to you, "I have bought a new book for you the day before."
explanation: Indirect mein "had bought" (Past Perfect) hai. Direct mein Past Indefinite ("bought") hoga. "the day before" ka direct roop "yesterday" hota hai. Pronoun 'I' aur 'you' apni jagah theek hain. Option 1 bilkul spot on hai.

set28.word: Shikha said, "The heavy rain this week has spoiled all my travel plans."
options: Shikha said that the heavy rain that week had spoiled all her travel plans.
Shikha told me that the heavy rain that week had spoiled all her travel plans.
Shikha said that the heavy rain that week has spoiled all her travel plans.
Shikha said that the heavy rain this week had spoiled all her travel plans.
explanation: "this week" (Time word) change hoga "that week" mein. "has spoiled" (Present Perfect) change hoga "had spoiled" (Past Perfect) mein. "my" (1st Person) Shikha ke hisaab se "her" banega. Option 1 in sabko accurately change kar raha hai.

set28.word: My brother said to me, "Don't play in the rain."
options: My brother said to me don't play in the rain.
My brother forbade me to play in the rain.
My brother assured me not to play in the rain.
My brother refused me not to play in the rain.
explanation: "Don't" (Negative command) ke do tarike hote hain: 1. "warned/advised me not to play" 2. "forbade me to play". "Forbade" (forbid ka past) ka matlab hi hota hai 'mana karna/rokna'. Kyunki iske andar already 'not' chhupa hota hai, hum uske aage negative nahi lagate, sirf "to play" jodd dete hain. Option 2 correct grammatical form hai.

set28.word: "So help me heaven!", the man cried, "I shall never lie again."
options: The man cries out to heaven to help him not to lie again ever.
The man called upon heaven to witness his resolve that he would never lie again.
The man requested heaven to help him and see that he lie again.
The man called heaven and ordered to help him and see to it that she never lies again.
explanation: "So help me heaven" ek kasam khane (oath) jaisa muhawara hai, iska literal matlab help mangna nahi hota balki "Bhagwan gawah hai" type ka matlab hota hai. Isliye "called upon heaven to witness" (gawah banane ke liye bulana) is emotion ka ekdum perfect indirect translation hai.

set28.word: I warned Rita not to be late the next day.
options: I warned to Rita, "Don't be late tomorrow".
I warning Rita, "Not be late tomorrow".
I warned Rita, "Not be late tomorrow".
I warned Rita, "Don't be late tomorrow".
explanation: Yeh Indirect se Direct banane ka case hai. "not to be late" (indirect) ka direct "Don't be late" banega. "the next day" (indirect) direct mein "tomorrow" hoga. Aur "warned to" nahi hota, sirf "warned" likhte hain bahar (halaanki option mein direct speech puchi gayi hai, jo I said/warned Rita ke form mein hai). Option 4 ekdum saaf aur sahi hai.

set29.word: My sister suggested that we go for a walk in the fresh air.
options: My sister said, "We shall go for a walk in the fresh air."
My sister said, "Go for a walk in the fresh air."
My sister said, "Let me go for a walk in the fresh air."
My sister said, "Let us go for a walk in the fresh air."
explanation: Jab bhi indirect mein "suggested/proposed that we/they should/go..." aata hai, uski Direct speech humesha "Let us..." (Let's) se shuru hoti hai. Kyunki "Let's" ka use hum koi kaam milkar karne ka suggestion dene ke liye karte hain. Option 4 exact rule match karta hai.

set29.word: The librarian said, "Let no student be issued a book till next week."
options: The librarian said that no student was to be issued a book till the following week.
The librarian said that no student is to be issued a book till next week.
The librarian said that no student will be issued a book till the following week.
The librarian said that let no student be issued a book till next week.
explanation: Jab "Let" ka use permission ya order deny karne ke liye ho ("Let no one..."), toh indirect mein usko "should not" ya "was/were not to be" se banate hain. "next week" banega "the following week". Option 1 mein tense (was to be) aur time word dono properly change hue hain.

set29.word: The teacher says, "Every action has an equal and opposite reaction."
options: The teacher said that every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
The teacher says every action had an equal and opposite reaction.
The teacher says that every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
The teacher said that every action had an equal and opposite reaction.
explanation: Golden rules yaad aayein? 1. Bahar "says" (Present) hai, toh tense change nahi hoga. 2. Yeh quote Newton's 3rd law (Universal Fact) hai, toh kisi bhi condition mein tense change nahi hoga! Bas comma hata kar 'that' lagana hai. Option 3 is correct.

set29.word: Harry said to me, "Don't wear this expensive watch to school."
options: Harry told me to not wear this expensive watch to school.
Harry told me that don't wear that expensive watch to school.
Harry told me not to wear that expensive watch to school.
Harry told me that not to wear that expensive watch to school.
explanation: Imperative negative ("Don't") ko indirect mein "not to + V1" rakhte hain (not to wear). "this" banega "that". Option 3 sab rules check karta hai.

set29.word: Her parents asked her if the match proposed by them would be acceptable to her.
options: Her parents said her, "Will the match proposed by us be acceptable to you?"
Her parents said her, "Would the match proposed by them be acceptable to her?"
Her parents said to her, "Will the match proposed by us be acceptable to you?"
Her parents said to her, "Was the match proposed by them acceptable to her?"
explanation: Indirect mein "asked if" (Yes/No question) hai. 'would be' past hai, toh direct mein future 'will be' hoga. Pronouns: "them" direct mein parents ke liye "us" hoga, "her" direct mein beti ke liye "you" hoga. Option 3: "Will the match proposed by us be acceptable to you?" sab badlaav sahi kar raha hai. (Option 1 mein 'said to' ki jagah sirf 'said' hai jo galat hai).

set29.word: The old man said, "I was walking in my garden at six o'clock."
options: The old man said that he had been walking in his garden at six o'clock.
The old man said that I had been walking in my garden at six o'clock.
The old man said that I was been walking in my garden at six o'clock.
The old man said that he was walking in his garden at six o'clock.
explanation: "was walking" (Past Continuous) ko badalna hai Past Perfect Continuous ("had been walking") mein. 'I' banega 'he', aur 'my' banega 'his'. Option 1 ekdum point par hai.

set29.word: The commander ordered the soldiers to march ahead and not to think of their enemy's large numbers.
options: The commander said to the soldiers, "March ahead. Do not think of your enemy's large numbers."
The commander said to the soldiers, "March ahead. Do not think of their enemy's large numbers."
The commander said to the soldiers, "Please march ahead. Do not think of your enemy's large numbers."
The commander said to the soldiers, "March ahead and not think of their enemy's large numbers."
explanation: Yeh indirect speech hai ek order ki. Direct mein commander apne soldiers ko directly command dega: "March ahead. Do not think...". 'their enemy's' direct mein soldiers ke reference se 'your enemy's' hoga. Commander 'Please' (Option 3) nahi bolega. Option 1 theek hai.

set29.word: The Chief Minister said, "All exams shall be cancelled this year."
options: The Chief Minister said that all exams should have been cancelled that year.
The Chief Minister said that all exams should be cancelled that year.
The Chief Minister said that all exams would be cancelled that year.
The Chief Minister says that all exams shall be cancelled this year.
explanation: Yahan "shall" ek future action bata raha hai kisi order/firm decision ke roop mein. 3rd person (exams) ke sath jab 'shall' ho, toh indirect mein wo aam taur par "would" ban jata hai (Kyuki should ka matlab aadesh/advice ban jata hai jo meaning badal dega). "this year" ka "that year" hoga. Option 3 is best.

set29.word: The little boy asked his teacher if she had always been good as a child.
options: The little boy said to his teacher, "Ma'am, always you were good as a child?"
The little boy said to his teacher, "You have always been good as a child?"
The little boy said to his teacher, "Ma'am, were you always good as a child?"
The little boy said to his teacher, "Are you always good as a child?"
explanation: Indirect hai. Tense hai "had been" (Past Perfect). Yani direct mein 'was/were' (Past Indefinite) raha hoga. Question "if" se shuru hai, toh Helping Verb aage aayegi: "were you...". Option 3 ka structure "Ma'am, were you always good..." bilkul direct speech jaisa natural sawal hai.

set29.word: Harsh said, "How happy I am to receive the best student award!"
options: Harsh exclaimed happily that I am very happy to receive the best student award.
Harsh exclaimed happily that he was receiving the best student award.
Harsh exclaimed with joy that how happy he was to receive the best student award.
Harsh exclaimed with joy that he was very happy to receive the best student award.
explanation: "How happy I am" Exclamatory hai. Ise assertive banayenge: "I am very happy". Phir tense badlenge: "was very happy". Subject: he. Bahar ki exclaiming verb: "exclaimed with joy". Option 4 in saari steps ko bakhubi poora karta hai.

set30.word: The investigator asked me, "Did you see or hear anything in the dead of night?"
options: The investigator asked me if I saw or heard anything in the dead of night.
The investigator asked me if I see or hear anything in the dead of night.
The investigator asked me if I had seen or heard anything in the dead of night.
The investigator asked me that if I had seen or heard anything in the dead of night.
explanation: Question "Did" (Past Indefinite) se start hai. Past Indefinite badalta hai Past Perfect (had + V3) mein! Toh "Did you see or hear" banega "if I had seen or heard". (Option 4 mein 'that if' double conjunctions hain jo galat hai). Option 3 perfect grammar hai.

set30.word: The teacher ordered the students to go straight to their classrooms.
options: The teacher said to the students, "Please go straight to your classrooms."
"Go straight to their classrooms," the teacher said to the students.
The teacher said, "Students, to go straight to your classrooms."
"Go straight to your classrooms," the teacher said to the students.
explanation: Indirect order ko direct banana hai. Teacher students se seedha bolega: "Go straight to your classrooms." Option 4 mein yahi phrase theek se inverted commas mein rakha gaya hai (bina 'Please' ke, kyuki 'ordered' hai).

set30.word: The doctor exclaimed with relief that he didn't need to go out in the rain.
options: The doctor said with relief, "He didn't needed to go out in the rain."
The doctor said relieved, "I needn't to go out in the rain."
The doctor said, "Thank God! I needn't go out in the rain."
The doctor exclaimed, "Oh, I don't have to go out in the rain!"
explanation: Indirect mein "didn't need to" (ya needn't) hai. Direct mein aam taur par modal 'needn't' use hota hai (jiske baad 'to' nahi lagta). "exclaimed with relief" (rahat ki saans li) dikhane ke liye direct speech mein "Thank God!" jaisa phrase sabse natural emotion capture karta hai. "needn't go" sahi grammar hai.

set30.word: The captain said that the crew had to leave the ship at Liverpool.
options: The captain said, "The crew has to leave the ship at Liverpool."
The captain said, "The crew have to leave the ship at Liverpool."
The captain said, "The crew could leave the ship at Liverpool."
The captain said to the crew, "Leave the ship at Liverpool."
explanation: Indirect mein "had to" (majboori) hai. Direct mein yeh present form "has to" ya "have to" raha hoga. Yahan "The crew" ko ek singular collective noun ki tarah treat kiya gaya hai, isliye iske sath "has to" (Option 1) aayega.
